title: Zeekr one-piece hot-stamped B-pillar + rocker sill tailored blank
description: Replace 4-piece welded B-pillar (3× 590MPa stampings + 1 reinforcement, 14 spot welds) with a single laser-welded tailored blank of 22MnB5 boron steel hot-stamped at 1500MPa, varying 1.2mm in the deformation zone to 1.8mm at the roof node. Eliminates 14 weld joints, 2 stamping dies (NRC −€1.1M) and 0.9kg/vehicle. Part cost drops from €23.40 to €17.10 (€6.30/veh), labour −1.4 min. Mirrors Zeekr 001 BIW.

title: Audi roll-formed 1500MPa rocker reinforcement
description: Swap a deep-drawn 780MPa DP rocker reinforcement (1.6mm, 38% material scrap on blanking) for a roll-formed closed section in 1500MPa martensitic steel at 1.2mm. Roll forming yields 97% material utilisation vs 62%, saving 1.1kg and €4.80/veh in raw coil. Tooling NRC for roll set is €240k vs €1.4M draw die. Section modulus rises 18% for side-impact. Benchmarks Audi Q6 e-tron sill.

title: Nio aluminium-extrusion front crash box with crush initiators
description: Replace a 3-part welded steel crash box (590MPa, 9 spot welds) with a 6063-T6 extruded octagonal tube, 2.5mm wall, with laser-cut crush beads tuned for 180kN peak. Extrusion + sawing replaces stamping and welding, removing 9 joints and a fixture (NRC −€180k). Bolted MIG-free attach to bumper beam saves 0.7 min/veh. Net −0.8kg, piece cost €19.10 vs €22.40 (€3.30/veh). Mirrors Nio ET5 longitudinal.

title: Mercedes door-in-white aluminium-steel hybrid architecture
description: Re-architect the front door-in-white: AA6014-T4 outer panel roll-hemmed to a 590MPa steel inner ring, replacing an all-steel 6-panel assembly (22 spot welds + 2 reinforcements). Self-pierce riveting and structural adhesive cut 22 welds to 16 SPR points, removing a reinforcement bracket. Mass −2.3kg, piece cost −€11.20/veh, one stamping die deleted (NRC −€900k). Benchmarks Mercedes EQE door build.

title: Porsche hot-stamped A-pillar with tailor-rolled blank
description: Convert a 2-piece A-pillar (980MPa AHSS + insert, 11 welds) to a single tailor-rolled 22MnB5 blank hot-stamped 1500MPa, continuously varying 1.0–2.1mm to delete the local insert. Removes 11 welds and one stamping. Mass −1.5kg, piece cost €26.80 vs €31.50 (€4.70/veh), labour −1.2 min. Improves roof-crush FMVSS216 margin 14%. Benchmarks Porsche Macan EV upper A-pillar.

title: Xpeng front gigacast shock-tower integration
description: Integrate front shock towers, strut housings and dash-toe nodes into a single AlSi10MnMg gigacast (3.5mm wall) replacing 38 stamped/welded parts and 210 joints. Two robot weld cells removed; BIW front-end takt −19%. Piece cost €132 vs €188 assembly (€56/veh), NRC amortised on 7000T die. Vacuum casting limits porosity for fatigue. Benchmarks Xpeng G6 front structure.

title: Volvo PA66-GF30 windscreen encapsulation injection-molded frame
description: Replace a separately extruded PVC windscreen surround plus 6 clips with an in-mould PA66-GF30 encapsulation injected directly onto the glass edge (2.5mm rib). Eliminates 6 clips, a primer step and 0.9 min manual fitment. Dimensional tolerance tightens to ±0.3mm improving flush/gap. Piece cost €7.10 vs €9.40 (€2.30/veh), tooling NRC €310k. Reduces wind-noise leak path. Benchmarks Volvo EX90 windscreen.

title: Li Auto multi-material BIW side frame with laser-welded blanks
description: Re-engineer the full bodyside outer as 3 laser-welded tailored blanks (180BH skin, 340LA quarter, 1180MPa hinge zone) instead of 7 discrete stampings with 31 welds. Consolidation deletes 3 dies (NRC −€1.6M) and 18 welds, with 24% less edge scrap. Mass −3.1kg, piece cost −€13.40/veh. Class-A retained via laser-seam grinding. Benchmarks Li Auto L9 bodyside outer.

title: BMW hydroformed roof bow cross-member
description: Replace a 2-shell welded roof bow (270MPa, 16 welds + 2 flanges) with a single hydroformed closed tube in 590MPa steel, 1.4mm wall. Hydroforming removes 16 welds, 2 stampings and the flange overlap, saving 0.8kg and improving torsional stiffness 11%. Piece cost €14.20 vs €17.30 (€3.10/veh), tool NRC €420k vs €980k for twin draw dies. Benchmarks BMW Neue Klasse roof structure.

title: Hongqi tailgate plastic-composite SMC outer panel
description: Substitute a steel tailgate outer (2 stampings, 12 welds) with a single compression-moulded SMC (low-density 1.3 g/cc, glass-fibre) panel bonded to a steel inner. Removes 1 stamping die (NRC −€780k) and 12 welds, dropping 2.7kg. SMC tolerates online paint at 180°C. Piece cost €21.50 vs €25.90 (€4.40/veh), no e-coat corrosion risk. Benchmarks Hongqi E-HS9 liftgate.

title: Denza co-extruded TPV glass-run channel with flocked lip
description: Replace EPDM dense-rubber glass-run channel with a co-extruded TPV (thermoplastic vulcanizate) profile, dense carrier + foamed sealing lip, flocked contact face. TPV is fully recyclable and extrudes 30% faster with no sulfur curing oven, cutting energy and scrap. Mass −0.4kg/door set, piece cost €4.30 vs €5.60 (€1.30/veh across 4 doors). Lower glass-drop friction. Benchmarks Denza N7 door glass run.

title: AITO rear floor-pan single-shot stamped deep-draw consolidation
description: Combine a 4-piece rear floor pan (270MPa, 26 welds, 2 spare-well inserts) into one deep-drawn 340LA panel with integrated spare well and seat-mount embossments. Deletes 3 stampings and 18 welds, saving 1.9kg of flange overlap. Piece cost €18.70 vs €23.10 (€4.40/veh), tool NRC €1.1M consolidates 4 dies into 1 progressive line. Benchmarks AITO M7 rear floor.

title: AVATR cast-aluminium rear gigacast underbody
description: Single AlSi10MnMg rear underbody gigacast (vacuum HPDC, 3.2mm wall) replacing 79 parts and 280 spot/MIG joints including rear rails and damper mounts. Removes 2 weld cells, cutting takt 21% and 16kg flange mass. Piece cost €144 vs €206 assembly (€62/veh); 8000T press die NRC amortised over 6 yr. Localised heat-treat-free alloy avoids quench distortion. Benchmarks AVATR 12 rear body.

title: Maextro 1180MPa cold-stamped dash cross-member
description: Upgrade the dash (cowl) cross-member from 590MPa DP at 1.8mm to 1180MPa CP steel at 1.2mm cold-stamped. Down-gauging saves 1.3kg and €5.10/veh raw steel while holding bending stiffness for steering-column mount. Existing dies reused with new draw beads (NRC €160k). Removes one reinforcement patch (−4 welds). Benchmarks Maextro S800 cowl cross-car beam.

title: Luxeed two-piece-to-one roll-formed roof rail
description: Convert a stamped 2-shell roof side rail (440MPa, 20 welds) to a roll-formed closed profile in 980MPa AHSS at 1.4mm. Roll forming reaches 95% utilisation vs 58%, saving €5.60/veh coil and 1.4kg. Two draw dies deleted (NRC −€1.3M), 20 welds cut to 8 closure welds. Improves roof-crush 16%. Benchmarks Luxeed S7 roof rail.

title: Yangwang adhesive-bonded panoramic glass roof module
description: Replace a steel roof panel + bolted glass sunroof cassette with a structural-bonded fixed panoramic laminated glass (4.0mm) directly on the roof ring via PU bead, deleting the cassette frame, 14 bolts and a seal cartridge. Saves 5.2kg and one stamping die (NRC −€820k). Piece cost €74 vs €96 (€22/veh) net of glass premium. Tightens flush ±0.4mm. Benchmarks Yangwang U8 glass roof.

title: BMW SPR-joined aluminium bonnet inner with reduced ribs
description: Re-design the bonnet inner from a deep-drawn AA5182 single panel (heavy rib pattern) to an optimised AA6016-T4 hexagonal-rib inner self-pierce-riveted to outer with structural adhesive, replacing 18 hem-bonded mastic pads. Topology cut removes 1.1kg while meeting pedestrian head-impact GTR9. Piece cost −€6.90/veh, 18 mastic stations deleted (−1.6 min). Benchmarks BMW i5 bonnet.

title: Zeekr integrated front-rail-to-shock-tower gigacast
description: Cast front longitudinal rails, shock towers and plenum lower into one AlSi10MnMg gigacast (3.4mm wall) replacing 36 stamped parts and 190 joints. Deletes 2 weld stations, dropping front-end takt 18% and 14kg. Piece cost €128 vs €182 (€54/veh); 7200T die NRC over 6 yr. Local annealing zones aid bolt-on crash-box repairability. Benchmarks Zeekr 007 front casting.

title: Mercedes laser-brazed roof-to-bodyside zero-gap joint
description: Replace a spot-welded roof ditch with applied moulding by a continuous CuSi3 laser-brazed roof-to-bodyside seam, eliminating the roof-ditch moulding, 2 clips and a sealant bead. Removes a 0.6m moulding part (€3.80) and 0.9 min fitment. Brazing speed 4 m/min reduces flange width 6mm, saving 0.5kg. Net −€4.60/veh. Improves Class-A appearance. Benchmarks Mercedes C-Class roof braze.

title: BYD CTB integrated floor-as-battery-lid structure
description: Adopt cell-to-body where the battery upper cover IS the vehicle floor pan, deleting a separate stamped floor (4 parts, 40 welds) and a pack lid. Honeycomb-sandwich blade-cell top sheet bonds to sills via structural adhesive. Removes 1 stamping line (NRC −€1.4M), 12kg and 3.5 min sealing labour. Net −€31/veh. Raises torsional stiffness 30%. Benchmarks BYD Seal CTB floor.

title: Audi co-extruded EPDM-foam body main weatherstrip on carrier-less clip
description: Replace a steel-carrier EPDM door aperture weatherstrip with a carrier-less co-extruded EPDM-sponge profile using moulded push-in fir-tree clips, deleting the embedded metal carrier and 100% of clip-on labour on the robot apply cell. Mass −0.6kg/car, raw cost −€2.10/veh across full aperture. Sealing force tuned to 8N/100mm for door-close effort. Benchmarks Audi A6 e-tron primary seal.

title: Porsche cast-magnesium front strut shock-tower
description: Replace a 5-piece welded steel shock tower (590MPa, 22 welds) with a single AM60B magnesium die-casting, 4.0mm wall. Consolidation removes 22 welds, 4 stampings and a fixture (NRC −€760k), shedding 2.8kg. Mg HPDC piece cost €34 vs €28 steel assembly but deletes €11/veh labour+joining, net −€5/veh. Local steel insert for strut-bolt fatigue. Benchmarks Porsche Panamera tower.

title: Nio bonded full door-in-white aluminium ring frame
description: Build the rear door-in-white as an AA6014 inner ring SPR-bonded to AA6014-T4 outer with a 1500MPa hot-stamped anti-intrusion beam, replacing an all-steel 7-panel door (26 welds, 2 reinforcements). 26 welds become 20 SPR + adhesive; one die deleted (NRC −€880k). Mass −3.4kg, piece cost −€12.80/veh. Benchmarks Nio ET7 rear door build.

title: Li Auto roll-formed 1500MPa front bumper beam
description: Convert a hot-stamped B-section bumper beam (1500MPa, 2-piece, 10 welds) to a roll-formed closed octagon in 1500MPa martensitic at 1.6mm, sweep-bent to bumper radius. Roll forming removes the hot-stamp furnace energy and 10 welds, with 96% utilisation. Mass −1.0kg, piece cost €21.30 vs €26.10 (€4.80/veh), tool NRC €380k. Benchmarks Li Auto L7 front beam.

title: Hongqi gigacast rear floor with integrated rear rails
description: Cast the rear floor, rear rails and tower mounts as one AlSi10MnMg gigacast (3.5mm wall) replacing 68 parts and 240 joints. Two weld cells and 15kg of flanges removed; takt −20%. Piece cost €140 vs €198 (€58/veh); 8000T die NRC over 7 yr. Crash zones tuned via wall-thickness grading. Benchmarks Hongqi EH7 rear underbody.

title: Volvo down-gauged 1180MPa CP A-pillar lower
description: Re-grade the A-pillar lower from 780MPa DP at 1.9mm to 1180MPa CP at 1.3mm, down-gauging while holding small-overlap crash load path. Saves 1.2kg and €4.70/veh raw steel. Existing draw die reworked for new springback (NRC €150k). Pairs with a deleted local reinforcement (−6 welds). Benchmarks Volvo EX30 A-pillar lower.

title: AITO TPE bonded fixed-glass body sealing system
description: Consolidate quarter-glass, C-pillar trim seal and drip rail into one robot-applied TPE encapsulation bonded to flush fixed glass, deleting 2 EPDM strips, 8 clips and a butyl bead. Removes 1.1 min manual fitment and 0.7kg. Flush glazing cuts Cd by 0.004. Piece cost €9.80 vs €13.20 (€3.40/veh). Benchmarks AITO M9 rear quarter glazing.

title: AVATR 980MPa laser-welded blank floor cross-member
description: Replace a 3-piece seat cross-member (440MPa, 16 welds) with a laser-welded tailored blank (980MPa Docol at seat anchors, 340LA mid-span) stamped as one part. Deletes 2 stampings, 10 welds and a fixture (NRC −€420k). Mass −0.9kg, piece cost €13.60 vs €16.90 (€3.30/veh). Meets seat-belt-anchor pull. Benchmarks AVATR 11 floor cross-member.

title: Xpeng aluminium-extrusion sill with internal crush ribs
description: Replace a multi-cell steel rocker (3× 1180MPa stampings, 28 welds) with a 6082-T6 multi-void extruded sill, 3.0mm webs, protecting the battery side in pole impact. Extrusion deletes 28 welds and 3 dies (NRC −€1.5M), saving 2.6kg. Bolted/bonded to floor cuts 2.1 min weld time. Piece cost €38 vs €33 steel but −€14/veh labour+joining, net −€9/veh. Benchmarks Xpeng G9 sill.

title: Maextro PA66-GF35 active grille shutter glazing surround
description: Replace an aluminium windscreen lower cowl seal retainer with an injection-moulded PA66-GF35 retainer integrating drain channels and the cowl-to-glass seal lip in one shot. Deletes a metal bracket, 4 screws and a foam dam. Mass −0.5kg, piece cost €5.40 vs €7.30 (€1.90/veh), tool NRC €280k. Improves water management. Benchmarks Maextro S800 cowl seal.

title: Luxeed full BIW floor multi-material laser-welded architecture
description: Re-architect the floor assembly using 5 laser-welded tailored blanks (boron tunnel, 590MPa pans, 1180MPa anchors) replacing 19 stampings and 64 welds. Consolidation deletes 4 dies (NRC −€1.9M), 22 welds and 4.0kg flange overlap. Continuous laser seams seal better, cutting sealer 1.8 min. Net −€16/veh. Benchmarks Luxeed R7 floor.

title: Denza hot-stamped one-piece B-pillar outer + reinforcement
description: Integrate B-pillar outer and reinforcement into a single tailor-rolled 22MnB5 hot-stamped part (1500MPa, 1.0–1.9mm) with a soft-zone at the lower base for controlled folding, replacing 3 parts and 18 welds. Deletes 2 dies (NRC −€1.2M), 1.6kg and 14 welds. Piece cost €24.90 vs €30.40 (€5.50/veh). Benchmarks Denza D9 B-pillar.

title: BMW aluminium gigacast rear floor (Neue Klasse)
description: Replace a stamped-welded rear floor module (66 parts, 230 joints) with one AlSi10MnMg gigacast, 3.3mm wall, integrating rear rails and damper towers. Removes 2 weld cells, 15kg and cuts takt 20%. Piece cost €138 vs €196 (€58/veh); 8500T die NRC over 7 yr. Self-piercing repair patches preserve serviceability. Benchmarks BMW Neue Klasse rear casting.

title: Mercedes 6016-T4 aluminium fender with integrated bracket
description: Replace a steel fender (270BH) plus 3 bolted steel mounting brackets with a single AA6016-T4 fender with stamped-in mounting flanges, deleting 3 brackets, 9 bolts and 0.8 min assembly. Mass −1.7kg, piece cost €16.40 vs €19.20 incl. brackets (€2.80/veh). Aluminium avoids stone-chip corrosion. Benchmarks Mercedes EQS fender.

title: BYD cold-formed 1500MPa door anti-intrusion beam
description: Switch the door side-impact beam from a hot-stamped tube (1500MPa) to a roll-formed 1500MPa martensitic tube at 1.4mm, removing the hot-stamp furnace energy and quench die. Roll forming cuts piece cost €8.90 vs €12.10 (€3.20/veh across 4 doors) and 0.6kg. Meets FMVSS214 intrusion. Tool NRC €240k. Benchmarks BYD Han door beam.

title: Audi structural-foam-filled A-pillar node
description: Replace a welded-in steel reinforcement bracket at the A-pillar/hinge node with an expandable epoxy structural-foam insert baked during e-coat, restoring stiffness and NVH without the stamping. Deletes 1 bracket (€2.60), 6 welds and 0.5 min. Foam insert €0.90, net −€2.10/veh and −0.4kg. Improves body-bending mode 6Hz. Benchmarks Audi Q4 e-tron pillar node.

title: Porsche full aluminium spaceframe side-frame with cast nodes
description: Build the bodyside as extruded 6082-T6 rails joined by AlSi10MnMg cast nodes (A-pillar, B-pillar, C-pillar) via MIG + SPR, replacing 9 steel stampings and 34 welds. Cast nodes consolidate junctions, deleting 5 dies (NRC −€2.2M) and 4.6kg. Net piece cost −€11/veh after labour. Benchmarks Porsche Taycan side spaceframe.

title: Nio flocked TPV inner-belt waist seal
description: Replace an EPDM inner-belt (waist) seal with stainless insert by a co-extruded TPV belt seal with a stamped-clip-less push-on carrier and flocked lip. TPV recyclable, no vulcanising oven, extrudes 25% faster. Mass −0.3kg/car, piece cost €3.10 vs €4.40 (€1.30/veh). Lower glass scrub noise. Benchmarks Nio ES6 belt seal.

title: Zeekr aluminium-casting integrated rear shock-tower + wheelhouse
description: Integrate rear shock tower, wheelhouse inner and rail node into one AlSi10MnMg casting (3.6mm wall) replacing 12 stampings and 58 welds. Deletes 3 dies (NRC −€1.3M) and 4.1kg. Casting bolts to extruded rails, cutting 2.4 min weld time. Net piece cost −€13/veh. Benchmarks Zeekr 009 rear tower casting.

title: Hongqi 1180MPa roll-formed dash cross-car beam
description: Replace a 2-shell stamped cowl beam (440MPa, 18 welds) with a roll-formed 1180MPa closed tube at 1.3mm. Roll forming reaches 95% utilisation, deletes 18 welds and 2 dies (NRC −€1.1M), saving 1.5kg. Higher torsional stiffness aids steering-column NVH. Piece cost €15.10 vs €18.40 (€3.30/veh). Benchmarks Hongqi H9 cowl beam.

title: Li Auto adhesive-bonded panoramic windscreen-to-roof glazing
description: Adopt a continuous bonded windscreen-into-roof panoramic laminate (4.2mm) deleting the front roof bow header, header trim seal and 2 mouldings, replaced by a PU structural bead to the cowl/cant rail. Removes 1 stamping (NRC −€620k), 2.9kg and 1.3 min sealing. Net −€14/veh after glass premium. Benchmarks Li Auto MEGA front glazing.

title: AITO 22MnB5 hot-stamped front rail with tailored soft-zone
description: Replace a 3-piece stamped front longitudinal (590MPa, 24 welds) with a hot-stamped 22MnB5 rail (1500MPa) with laser-quench-tuned soft zones for axial crush, integrating the front bulkhead flange. Deletes 2 dies (NRC −€1.2M), 18 welds and 2.0kg. Piece cost €27 vs €32 (€5/veh). Benchmarks AITO M5 front rail.

title: AVATR full BIW underbody central gigacast (front+mid)
description: Combine front and mid underbody into a single large AlSi10MnMg gigacast (3.4mm wall) replacing 90 parts and 300 joints across rails, tunnel and seat mounts. Removes 3 weld cells, 18kg and cuts BIW takt 24%. Piece cost €156 vs €218 (€62/veh); 9000T die NRC over 7 yr. Crush-grading via local wall control. Benchmarks AVATR 12 central casting.

title: Maextro cold-stamped 980MPa floor longitudinal reinforcement
description: Re-grade a floor longitudinal reinforcement from 590MPa DP at 1.8mm to 980MPa DP Docol at 1.3mm, down-gauging while holding crash load path. Saves 1.1kg and €4.20/veh raw steel. Existing die reused with new beads (NRC €140k). Pairs with deleted overlap patch (−4 welds). Benchmarks Maextro S800 floor reinforcement.

title: Luxeed injection-moulded door inner module carrier
description: Replace a steel door inner sub-panel plus 4 brackets with a PP-LGF40 injection-moulded inner module carrier integrating window-reg rails, speaker and wiring clips, bonded to the door ring, deleting 4 brackets, 11 fasteners and 1.4 min assembly. Mass −2.1kg, piece cost €18.30 vs €22.60 (€4.30/veh). Benchmarks Luxeed S7 door module.

title: Denza forged-aluminium door hinge with integrated check
description: Replace a 2-piece stamped-steel door hinge plus a separate bolted door-check link with one forged 6082 aluminium hinge integrating the detent check ramp, deleting the check bracket, 3 bolts and 0.6 min. Mass −0.5kg/hinge (−1.0kg/door pair). Forging piece cost €6.20 vs €8.40 incl. check (€2.20/veh per hinge). Benchmarks Denza N8 door hinge.

title: Yangwang gigacast front+shock-tower mega-casting
description: Cast the entire front structure (rails, shock towers, plenum, A-pillar lower nodes) as one AlSi10MnMg gigacast, 3.5mm wall, replacing 42 parts and 200 joints. Deletes 2 weld cells, 16kg and cuts takt 19%. Piece cost €146 vs €204 (€58/veh); 8000T die NRC over 7 yr. Bolt-on crash boxes for repair. Benchmarks Yangwang U9 front casting.

title: BMW co-extruded EPDM-TPV hybrid trunk aperture seal
description: Replace a moulded-corner EPDM trunk seal (4 vulcanised joints) with a continuous co-extruded EPDM-TPV profile using injection-moulded corners in-line, deleting 4 hand-glued joints and the corner-mould fixture (NRC −€180k). Mass −0.4kg, piece cost €5.10 vs €6.80 (€1.70/veh), 0.7 min saved. Better water-leak seal. Benchmarks BMW iX trunk seal.

title: Mercedes one-piece hot-stamped tunnel + tailored floor pan
description: Integrate the centre tunnel and front floor pans into one laser-welded tailored blank (1500MPa boron tunnel + 340LA pans) hot-stamped as a single part, replacing 5 stampings and 30 welds. Deletes 3 dies (NRC −€1.5M), 22 welds and 2.4kg overlap. Continuous seam improves NVH and sealing (−1.2 min sealer). Net −€11/veh. Benchmarks Mercedes EQE tunnel/floor.

title: Xpeng PA66-GF30 panoramic glass retention frame
description: Replace an aluminium panoramic-roof glass retention frame plus EPDM seal with an injection-moulded PA66-GF30 frame with a co-moulded TPE seal lip in one shot, bonded to the laminate. Deletes the metal frame, 8 clips and a separate seal (€4.10), saving 1.3kg. Piece cost €11.20 vs €15.60 (€4.40/veh), tool NRC €340k. Improves flush and acoustic seal. Benchmarks Xpeng G6 panoramic roof frame.

title: LFP blade cell migration from NMC622 prismatic
description: Replace NMC622 prismatic 120Ah cells with LFP blade cells (960mm long, 13.5mm thick). LFP eliminates cobalt and nickel, cutting cell cost from ~€105/kWh to ~€72/kWh. The blade form factor enables direct cell-to-pack stacking, removing module hardware. Energy density loss (160 vs 220 Wh/kg) is offset by 18% volumetric packaging gain via CTP integration. At 90kWh fleet this saves ~€33/kWh. Benchmarks BYD Han EV blade architecture.

title: Single-crystal NMC811 cathode for cycle-life uprate
description: Switch polycrystalline NMC811 to single-crystal cathode morphology. Single-crystal grains resist micro-cracking, extending cycle life from 1500 to 2800 cycles and removing the need for 8% oversizing that compensated for capacity fade. Eliminating the oversize buffer reduces active material per pack by ~6kg, saving ~€18/kWh. Lower gassing also permits thinner cell can walls. Benchmarks Nio 100kWh cell upgrade using single-crystal high-nickel cathode.

title: Silicon-carbon anode to shrink cell count
description: Introduce 6% silicon-carbon composite anode to lift cell capacity from 4.2Ah to 4.9Ah in 4680 cells. Higher gravimetric capacity (470 vs 360 mAh/g blend) cuts cell count per pack by 14%, removing ~120 cells, busbars, and welds per vehicle. Net BOM saving ~€55/vehicle on interconnects plus assembly time. Pre-lithiation manages first-cycle loss. Benchmarks Porsche/Group silicon anode cell development with Cellforce.

title: Sodium-ion entry cells for base-trim pack
description: Deploy sodium-ion cylindrical cells on the entry-trim 45kWh pack. Sodium-ion uses abundant Na and aluminium current collectors on both electrodes, removing copper foil (~€4/kWh) and cobalt entirely, targeting ~€65/kWh. Excellent low-temperature retention (90% at -20°C) reduces heater duty. Lower energy density (145 Wh/kg) is acceptable for short-range trim. Benchmarks BYD/CATL sodium-ion pack programs entering city EVs in 2024–2025.

title: Composite copper-PET current collector foil
description: Replace 6µm rolled copper anode foil with composite collector: 4.5µm PET film sputtered with 1µm copper on each side. Copper mass drops ~60%, saving ~€3.5/kWh in commodity content, and the polymer core acts as a fusible safety layer that interrupts internal shorts. Lower foil weight adds ~5 Wh/kg. Roll-to-roll plating scales to GWh. Benchmarks Zeekr cells trialling composite current collectors for energy density and safety.

title: Ceramic-coated polyolefin separator downgauge
description: Move from 16µm PE separator with 4µm double-side alumina coat to a 9µm PE base with 2µm boehmite single-side coat. Thinner, lighter separator reclaims ~3% jelly-roll volume for active material and cuts separator cost ~€1.8/kWh. Boehmite is cheaper than alumina and maintains 150°C shutdown integrity. Validated for thermal-runaway propagation resistance. Benchmarks Zeekr golden-brick LFP cell separator specification.

title: Dry-electrode coating to remove NMP solvent
description: Adopt solvent-free dry-electrode calendering (PTFE-fibrillated binder) for cathode coating, eliminating NMP slurry, the 80m drying ovens, and solvent-recovery plant. Energy per cell falls ~30% and capex for coating lines drops ~€15M amortised to ~€7/kWh. Thicker electrodes (>4 mAh/cm²) also raise energy density. Yields stabilising in 2024 pilots. Benchmarks Tesla 4680 dry-cathode rollout informing Group cell strategy.

title: LMFP cathode blend for range without nickel
description: Upgrade LFP to LMFP (lithium manganese iron phosphate, ~Mn0.6Fe0.4) lifting cell voltage from 3.2V to 3.7V and energy density ~15% to 200 Wh/kg. Keeps the cobalt/nickel-free cost base (~€74/kWh) while closing range gap, avoiding a switch to costly NMC. Fewer cells needed for equal kWh trims interconnect content. Manganese dissolution mitigated by surface coating. Benchmarks Zeekr/CATL M3P-style manganese-phosphate cells.

title: Tabless 4680 electrode for fast-charge yield
description: Convert 21700 cells to tabless 4680 format. The full-height continuous current path cuts internal resistance ~5x, slashing I²R heat and allowing the thermal system to shed one chiller stage. Larger format halves cell count per pack, removing ~50% of welds, wire bonds, and holder parts, saving ~€40/vehicle in interconnects. Cell-level energy density rises via reduced inactive mass. Benchmarks BMW Gen6 large cylindrical cell program.

title: Cell vent valve laser-scored membrane
description: Replace the riveted burst-disc vent on prismatic cells with a laser-scored aluminium membrane integrated into the cell lid stamping. Eliminates a separate disc, gasket, and crimp operation (3 parts, 1 station) at ~€0.18/cell, scaling to meaningful savings at 8M cells/year. Scoring depth tuned for 1.0MPa burst keeps directional venting away from neighbours. Benchmarks BYD blade cell integrated top-vent design.

title: Wire-bond to laser-weld busbar interconnect
description: Replace aluminium wire-bonding of cylindrical cells with laser-welded stamped busbars and a fusible nickel link. Wire bonding runs ~1.2s per bond across thousands of bonds; stamped busbar welding processes a full row in one shot, cutting takt ~40% and removing the bonder consumables. Busbar also lowers interconnect resistance. Saves ~€9/module in cycle time and scrap. Benchmarks Tesla structural pack busbar welding adopted by Volvo cell lines.

title: Module endplate from glass-filled PA to HPDC
description: Switch the two module compression endplates from machined 6082 aluminium to high-pressure die-cast ADC12. Casting nets net-shape features (locating bosses, tie-rod seats) in one shot, eliminating 6 machining operations and 30% material scrap. Per-endplate cost falls from ~€7.40 to ~€4.10. Stiffness maintained via ribbing for cell swelling preload of 4kN. Benchmarks Mercedes EQ module endplate casting conversion.

title: Foam-in-place cell spacer for swelling control
description: Replace die-cut PU compression pads between prismatic cells with dispensed foam-in-place 2K polyurethane. FIP eliminates pad tooling, manual pick-and-place, and 12% positional scrap, while delivering tuned compression curves for 350µm of charge swelling. Robotic dispense cuts ~€3.10/module versus pre-cut pads. Foam doubles as a minor thermal break. Benchmarks Li Auto module assembly using dispensed compression foam.

title: Module-to-CTP conversion eliminating module shell
description: Adopt cell-to-pack (CTP) architecture, deleting the intermediate module layer: endplates, side rails, module busbars, wiring harnesses and fasteners. Cells bond directly into the tray with structural adhesive. Volumetric efficiency rises from 55% to 72%, and part count drops ~40% (~360 fewer parts/pack), saving ~€55/vehicle in hardware and assembly. Benchmarks BYD Blade CTP and CATL Qilin pack architecture.

title: Cell-to-body integration of pack into floor
description: Move from cell-to-pack to cell-to-body (CTB), where cells bond to a sealed floor that doubles as the pack upper cover and structural body member. Removes the separate pack lid and a redundant sealing interface, and the body floor replaces the pack top plate, saving ~€48/vehicle in panels and 14kg. Torsional stiffness rises ~30%. FIPG silicone seals the cell array to the body. Benchmarks BYD CTB Seal platform.

title: HPDC ADC12 single-piece pack tray
description: Consolidate the welded aluminium-extrusion-and-stamping tray (38 parts) into a single high-pressure die-cast ADC12 underbody tray. Megacasting removes ~120 weld joints and their leak-test rework, cutting tray assembly labour ~50% and saving ~€70/vehicle. Integrated cooling channels and mounting bosses cast in net-shape. Requires a 6000T press. Benchmarks Zeekr/Tesla gigacast underbody and battery tray integration.

title: Steel-intensive tray for cost-down base pack
description: Replace the aluminium extrusion tray with a roll-formed and stamped dual-phase steel (DP780) enclosure on volume trims. Steel BOM is ~€2.10/kg versus ~€3.40/kg for aluminium and uses existing body-shop spot-welding rather than friction-stir, saving ~€38/vehicle and capex. ~22kg mass penalty accepted on base range. Cathodic e-coat handles corrosion. Benchmarks Volvo/Geely SEA steel-hybrid pack enclosures.

title: FIPG silicone replacing cured-gasket pack seal
description: Switch the pack lid sealing from a molded EPDM gasket to robot-dispensed Formed-In-Place-Gasket (FIPG) silicone. FIPG removes the molded-gasket tool, eliminates groove machining on the flange, and adapts to flange tolerance, cutting IP67 leak-test failures ~35%. Material and labour net ~€4.20/pack saving. Re-openability via cut-and-reapply. Benchmarks Mercedes pack sealing line using dispensed silicone bead.

title: Self-piercing rivet swap for FDS pack fasteners
description: Replace flow-drill screws joining the tray frame to the cover flange with self-piercing rivets (SPR). SPR cycle time is ~1.5s versus ~4s for FDS and needs no pre-heat or thread-forming, lifting line throughput and removing screw cost. At ~180 joints/pack the cycle and consumable delta saves ~€3.80/pack. No swarf contamination risk in the cell zone. Benchmarks Audi PPE pack frame joining strategy.

title: Stamped cold plate to roll-bond aluminium
description: Convert the brazed two-sheet stamped cooling plate to a roll-bond aluminium plate where channels are inflated from a screen-printed graphite pattern. Roll-bond removes the vacuum-braze furnace and its flux, cutting per-plate cost from ~€16 to ~€10 and improving channel-geometry freedom for uniform ΔT<3°C. Lighter by ~0.8kg. Benchmarks Nio roll-bond cooling plate used under blade-style modules.

title: Immersion cooling loop replacing cold plates
description: Replace bottom cold plates plus thermal gap-filler with single-phase dielectric immersion of cells in a circulated fluorinated/ester coolant. Direct contact removes the gap-filler (~€9/pack at 4kg) and equalises cell temperature, cutting peak ΔT to <2°C and enabling sustained 4C fast charge without a second chiller. Pump and dielectric add cost but net BOM and warranty improve. Benchmarks Yangwang U-series immersion-cooled blade pack.

title: Refrigerant-direct pack chiller deleting LCC loop
description: Adopt direct-refrigerant (R1234yf) cooling in the pack cold plate, deleting the secondary glycol loop, chiller heat exchanger, coolant pump, and expansion tank. Removing the intermediate loop eliminates ~7 components and ~€42/vehicle while improving cooling response by ~25% via direct evaporation. Electronic expansion valve manages superheat. Benchmarks Zeekr 800V pack with direct-cooling thermal architecture.

title: Gap-filler downgauge via cell-bottom cooling
description: Re-route cooling to cell base contact and cut thermally-conductive gap-filler from a 3mm full-coverage bead (4.0kg) to localized 1.5mm pads. Gap-filler is ~€11/kg dispensed; halving mass saves ~€22/pack and reduces dispense takt. Cell-bottom cooling via blade geometry keeps junction ΔT in spec. Lower filler mass also eases end-of-life disassembly. Benchmarks BYD blade bottom-cooling thermal layout.

title: Heat-pump-shared chiller for pack thermal loop
description: Integrate pack preconditioning into the vehicle CO2/R744 heat pump rather than a dedicated PTC battery heater. Waste heat recovery warms the pack for fast charge, cutting heater energy ~40% and deleting the 4kW PTC element and its HV harness (~€28/vehicle). Improves winter charge acceptance and range. Octovalve coordinates flows. Benchmarks Tesla octovalve-shared thermal system adopted in Volvo EX90.

title: Wireless BMS removing slave-board harness
description: Replace wired daisy-chain cell-monitoring with 2.4GHz wireless BMS (wBMS) per module. Removes the isoSPI/CAN harness, connectors, and ~30m of wiring per pack, cutting ~€26/vehicle and 1.2kg, while freeing routing for CTP layouts. Reduces assembly faults from connector mating. Functional-safety via redundant RF channels. Benchmarks GM/ADI wBMS informing Mercedes next-gen pack electronics.

title: BMS slave board channel consolidation
description: Upgrade cell-monitoring ASIC from 12-channel to 18-channel parts, reducing slave boards per pack from 8 to 6. Two fewer PCBAs removes connectors, isoSPI transceivers, and assembly, saving ~€14/pack and shrinking the harness. Higher channel count suits long LFP blade strings. Diagnostics and balancing current preserved. Benchmarks Li Auto pack BMS slave consolidation on its 800V platform.

title: Passive-balancing resistor uprate cuts balance time
description: Increase passive-balancing bleed from 60mA to 150mA by selecting lower-value power resistors on the slave board, avoiding a switch to costly active balancing ICs. Faster end-of-charge balancing reclaims usable capacity equivalent to ~1.5% pack energy, deferring cell oversizing. Board thermal handled by copper pour. Net saves ~€6/pack versus active balancing. Benchmarks Xpeng BMS balancing strategy on LFP packs.

title: Integrated BMS master into VCU domain controller
description: Fold the standalone BMS master controller into the central power-domain controller, sharing the MCU, power supply, and CAN/Ethernet transceivers. Eliminates a separate ECU housing, connector set, and harness branch (~€21/vehicle) and reduces software-integration variants. Cell-monitoring slaves remain local. ASIL-D partitioning maintained in the consolidated SoC. Benchmarks Xpeng/Zeekr zonal architecture absorbing BMS master functions.

title: Stamped busbar HV distribution replacing cables
description: Replace flexible HV cables between cell array, BDU, and connectors with laser-cut, formed aluminium busbars on an insulated carrier. Busbars remove crimped lugs, heat-shrink, and manual routing, cutting ~€19/vehicle and 0.9kg versus shielded cable, with lower resistance for 500A continuous. Snap-fit carrier speeds install. Benchmarks BMW Gen5 BDU busbar-based HV distribution.

title: Bistable contactor swap for economized coil type
description: Replace continuously-energized monostable main contactors with bistable (latching) gas-filled contactors that draw coil power only during switching. Eliminates ~6W standby per contactor (two per pack), saving parasitic energy and removing the economizer circuit on the driver board. Lower self-heating extends contact life. Net hardware and energy save ~€11/vehicle. Benchmarks Nio swap-station packs using latching HV contactors.

title: Pyro-fuse to solid-state HV disconnect
description: Replace the single-shot pyrotechnic disconnect plus separate fuse with a resettable solid-state SiC power switch performing both overcurrent interruption and crash disconnect. Removes the pyro squib, its igniter harness, and the melt-fuse, and eliminates field replacement after a trip, saving ~€15/vehicle plus warranty. Microsecond interruption improves short-circuit protection. Benchmarks AVATR/Huawei DriveONE solid-state HV protection.

title: Integrated BDU consolidating contactor and fuse
description: Consolidate main/precharge contactors, current sensor, MSD, and fuses into one molded battery disconnect unit with an integrated busbar frame. Single housing removes inter-component cabling, individual brackets, and 14 fasteners, cutting ~€24/vehicle and assembly time. Shared shunt simplifies HV measurement. Improves sealing to IP6K9K. Benchmarks Denza/BYD integrated BDU module. Single sealed unit also eases pack-level HV servicing and diagnostics.

title: HVIL loop simplification via daisy-chained connectors
description: Re-architect the High-Voltage Interlock Loop from a star topology to a single series daisy-chain through connector-integrated HVIL pins, removing dedicated interlock wires and the standalone monitoring relay. Cuts ~8 low-voltage wires and a relay (~€7/vehicle) while keeping unplug detection. Diagnostics read loop continuity at the BMS. Benchmarks Audi PPE HVIL series-loop implementation.

title: Aerogel inter-cell barrier downgauge
description: Optimize inter-cell thermal-runaway barriers from 3mm full silica aerogel to a 1.5mm aerogel-mica laminate. The hybrid retains <600°C cold-face under single-cell runaway while halving costly aerogel content (~€90/m²), saving ~€16/pack. Mica adds dielectric and mechanical robustness. Validated against no-propagation requirements. Benchmarks Zeekr/CATL Qilin no-propagation barrier design. Lower flammable mass also marginally reduces total pack thermal load during abuse events.

title: Mica top-cover sheet replacing potting compound
description: Replace ceramic potting over the cell-vent zone with a die-cut muscovite mica sheet bonded to the underside of the pack cover. Mica withstands >1000°C vent ejecta and installs in one pick-place, eliminating the messy potting dispense and cure station, saving ~€12/pack and takt. Also electrically insulates the cover. Benchmarks BYD blade pack mica top-shield.

title: Directional venting channel cast into tray
description: Integrate a directional venting plenum into the HPDC tray casting so runaway gases route to a single explosion vent, removing the bolted-on sheet-metal venting duct and its gaskets. Cast channels delete ~9 parts and their sealing, saving ~€13/pack, and improve flow to keep internal pressure below cover-burst. Single membrane vent remains. Benchmarks Zeekr cast-in venting architecture.

title: Single explosion vent valve replacing multi-vent
description: Consolidate four small pressure-equalization vents plus one burst vent into a single dual-function vent valve that breathes for altitude/temperature and bursts at 25kPa during runaway. Removes 4 vent assemblies and their seals (~€6/pack) and reduces IP-test leak points. Validated for water ingress and gas discharge. Benchmarks AITO/Luxeed pack venting consolidation.

title: Intumescent fire barrier coating on pack cover
description: Replace a bonded ceramic-fiber fire blanket under the pack cover with a sprayed intumescent coating that chars and expands at 200°C. Spray application removes the cut-blanket part, its adhesive, and lay-up labour, saving ~€9/pack and 0.6kg while meeting the 5-minute no-flame-penetration requirement. Uniform coverage on complex covers. Benchmarks Mercedes pack fire-protection coating trials.

title: Structural cell-to-pack bonding for floor stiffness
description: Use structural adhesive to bond blade cells between tray floor and cover so the cell array carries shear load (honeycomb effect), allowing deletion of internal cross-beams and reducing tray wall thickness. Removes ~8 cross-members (~€31/vehicle, 11kg) while raising pack bending stiffness ~25%. Adhesive doubles as thermal path. Benchmarks CATL Qilin/BYD blade load-bearing cell integration.

title: Extruded crash rail replacing welded box section
description: Replace the multi-piece welded steel side crash structure with a single 6082-T6 aluminium multi-cavity extrusion. The extrusion delivers tuned crush in one profile, removing ~14 stampings and ~60 spot welds, cutting ~€26/vehicle and rework. Multi-cell cavities absorb side-pole intrusion protecting cells. Benchmarks Porsche/PPE pack side-extrusion crash member. Recyclable single-alloy profile further lowers end-of-life processing cost versus mixed steel.

title: Constrained-layer damping patch reduction via tuned ribs
description: Remove bonded constrained-layer damping patches from the pack cover by casting tuned stiffening ribs that shift the first cover mode above 250Hz, away from road-noise excitation. Deletes ~1.2m² of damping material (~€8/vehicle) and the manual application step while meeting interior NVH targets. Modal validated by shaker test. Benchmarks Audi pack-cover NVH rib optimization.

title: Cell terminal aluminium-clad-copper transition
description: Use ultrasonically clad aluminium-copper transition terminals so all-aluminium busbars can join copper cell tabs without bimetallic corrosion or costly all-copper busbars. Aluminium busbars cut conductor cost ~€2.30/kWh versus copper at equal ampacity. Clad joint avoids galvanic failure in humid conditions. Welds reliably to both metals. Benchmarks Zeekr busbar bimetallic terminal design.

title: Prismatic 173Ah cell upsizing to cut part count
description: Move from 100Ah to 173Ah prismatic LFP cells, reducing cell count for a 90kWh pack from ~280 to ~162. Fewer cells means ~42% fewer busbars, welds, sense lines, and holders, saving ~€48/vehicle in interconnect and assembly while raising volumetric efficiency. Larger format keeps €/kWh flat. Thermal managed by bottom cooling. Benchmarks CATL/Denza large-format prismatic cell adoption.

title: Cell can material from steel to aluminium
description: Replace nickel-plated steel cylindrical cans with deep-drawn aluminium cans. Aluminium cuts cell mass ~12g per 4680 (~7 Wh/kg gain) and improves heat rejection from the can wall, reducing cooling demand. Avoids nickel plating line and its effluent treatment, netting ~€0.9/cell. Laser-weld closure validated. Benchmarks BMW Gen6 large-cylindrical aluminium-can cell. Single-alloy aluminium body also simplifies cell recycling and shredding recovery streams.

title: Single-piece stamped cooling-plate manifold
description: Integrate the coolant inlet/outlet manifold into the stamped cold-plate halves rather than welding on separate machined manifold blocks and barbed fittings. Forming the distribution gallery in-die removes 2 machined blocks, 4 brazed joints, and their leak risk per plate, saving ~€8/pack and a leak-test rework loop. Quick-connect ports overmolded. Benchmarks Li Auto integrated cold-plate manifold.

title: Module-less BMS sensing via flex-PCB cell connecting system
description: Replace discrete voltage/temperature sense wires and connectors with a single laser-structured flexible-PCB Cell Contacting System laminated to the busbar carrier. The FPC integrates sense traces, NTCs, and fusible links, deleting ~120 crimped wires per pack and their connectors, saving ~€18/vehicle and assembly faults. Suits CTP layouts. Benchmarks Mercedes/CATL flex-foil CCS in cell-to-pack designs.

title: Bonded composite pack underbody shield
description: Replace the bolted 3mm aluminium underbody impact shield with an adhesively-bonded glass-fiber SMC composite plate. SMC absorbs road-debris and ground-strike energy at lower mass (~4kg saved) and removes ~24 fasteners and their weld-nuts, cutting ~€15/vehicle and assembly time. Composite avoids galvanic corrosion at the tray interface. Benchmarks Porsche Taycan composite pack undershield.

title: 800V pack reconfiguration cutting conductor mass
description: Re-series the pack from 400V to 800V architecture, halving current for equal power and allowing busbar and HV-cable cross-section to drop ~50%. Lighter conductors save ~€21/vehicle in copper/aluminium and reduce I²R losses for ~1.5% range gain. Existing cells re-grouped, no chemistry change. Fast-charge to 5C enabled. Benchmarks Zeekr/AITO 800V platform conductor downsizing.

title: Laser-notched fusible busbar replacing discrete cell fuses
description: Integrate per-cell overcurrent protection by laser-notching a fusible neck directly into the stamped busbar connecting each cylindrical cell, removing discrete wire-fuses or fuse-bonds. The calibrated notch clears a shorted cell at ~30A, deleting thousands of separate fuse parts and bond operations, saving ~€7/vehicle and takt. Notch geometry validated by abuse test. Benchmarks Tesla fusible-busbar concept used at Volvo.

title: Reusable swap-pack standardized interface for scale
description: Standardize a swappable pack mechanical/electrical/coolant interface across vehicle lines so a single pack variant amortises tooling and qualification across higher volume. Shared latching busbar, blind-mate HV connector, and coolant quick-disconnect cut per-program engineering and enable battery-as-a-service. Standardization trims ~€55/vehicle via volume and reduced variants. Benchmarks Nio third-generation swap-station pack interface.

title: Continuous hairpin wave-winding to cut stator copper scrap
description: Replace discrete U-pin hairpin insertion with a continuous flat-wire wave-winding fed from a single coil, eliminating ~6 welding stations and cutting per-slot laser-weld joints from 96 to 8. Copper offcut scrap drops ~14%, raising slot fill from 62% to 73% and shaving ~0.18 kg copper per stator. At €9/kg copper plus reduced weld rework this saves ~€11/unit. Benchmarked against BMW Gen5 eDrive flat-wire stators.

title: Segmented stator core with bonded laminations vs interlocked
description: Switch from full-ring interlocked laminations to segmented (T-shaped) stator cores using thin 0.20 mm NO20 electrical steel with adhesive back-lack bonding. Segmentation lifts steel utilization from ~58% to ~88% on the press, recovering ~1.1 kg silicon steel per core, while back-lack reduces iron loss ~6%. Tooling cost drops on smaller progressive dies. Net ~€7/unit on material plus efficiency gain. Benchmarked against Mercedes eATS 2.0 segmented stator.

title: Thinner NO grade electrical steel for 800V high-frequency stator
description: Adopt 0.25 mm NO25 non-oriented steel in place of 0.35 mm grade for the 800V stator running at higher electrical frequency. Eddy-current iron loss falls ~22% at 16 kHz fundamental, allowing ~3% length reduction of the active stack for equal torque, trimming ~0.9 kg steel and ~0.12 kg copper per machine. Material premium is offset by stack shortening for ~€6/unit net. Benchmarked against Porsche Taycan PSM 800V stator.

title: Laser-skinned hairpin enamel removal replacing mechanical stripping
description: Replace mechanical/abrasive enamel stripping of flat-wire hairpin ends with galvo laser ablation, removing PEEK/PAI insulation in <0.4 s per pin with zero conductor nicking. Scrap from over-stripped pins drops from ~1.8% to ~0.3%, improving weld yield and removing one abrasive-media consumable line. Saves ~€3.20/stator plus reduced warranty on weld voids. Benchmarked against Audi PPE 800V hairpin stator lines.

title: Higher slot-fill via in-slot compaction for copper mass reduction
description: Apply post-insertion radial compaction of flat-wire conductors to lift slot fill from 68% to 78%, reducing DC winding resistance ~11% and copper loss at rated current. For equal efficiency the conductor cross-section is trimmed, cutting ~0.21 kg copper per stator (~€1.9/unit) and lowering cooling demand. Combined material plus efficiency credit ~€9/unit. Benchmarked against BYD e-Platform 3.0 flat-wire motor.

title: Single-component slot liner replacing multi-layer insulation
description: Consolidate the three-layer Nomex-Mylar-Nomex slot liner into a single calendered aramid-PET composite liner rated to 800V partial-discharge limits. Removes two insertion operations and one bill-of-material line, cutting insulation cost ~€2.10/stator and reducing slot wall thickness 0.15 mm to recover slot area for copper. Assembly cycle time drops ~4%. Benchmarked against Volvo/Volvo Cars EX90 dual-motor stator.

title: Ce-substituted NdFeB magnets to cut rare-earth content
description: Substitute ~20% of Nd/Pr with cerium in the rotor's NdFeB magnets (Ce-blended N48 grade) with grain-boundary diffusion of Dy only at pole edges. Reduces heavy/light rare-earth cost ~25% at ~€7/unit magnet-mass equivalent, with <3% remanence loss recovered via a 4% thicker pole geometry. Removes broad Dy dip-coating. Benchmarked against Nio 180/300 kW IPM rotor magnet strategy.

title: Grain-boundary-diffused Dy to cut bulk heavy rare-earth
description: Replace bulk dysprosium alloying with grain-boundary diffusion (GBD) of Dy/Tb only into the magnet surface, achieving the same coercivity at 150°C with ~60% less heavy rare earth. Dy usage falls from ~3.5% to ~1.3% by mass, saving ~€8/unit on magnet cost for a 2.4 kg magnet set. Maintains demagnetization margin at peak current. Benchmarked against Zeekr 800V 580 kW dual-motor rotor.

title: Delta-shaped double-V magnet topology reducing magnet mass
description: Adopt a double-V (delta) buried-magnet IPM topology that raises reluctance torque contribution from ~30% to ~45%, allowing ~12% reduction in NdFeB magnet mass for equal peak torque. Saves ~0.28 kg magnet (~€6/unit) and improves flux-weakening for high-speed 800V operation. Rotor lamination bridges retuned for mechanical margin at 18,000 rpm. Benchmarked against Xpeng X-EDU rear motor.

title: Carbon-fiber retaining sleeve replacing Inconel for high-speed rotor
description: Replace a press-fit Inconel/steel retaining ring with a filament-wound carbon-fiber sleeve for the surface-region magnets on a 22,000 rpm rotor. CFRP cuts rotating mass ~40% and removes a heavy nickel-alloy part, lowering bearing load and centrifugal stress so magnet bridges thin out. Net ~€9/unit including reduced balancing iterations. Benchmarked against Yangwang U9 high-speed drive rotor.

title: Two-plane automated rotor balancing to cut rework scrap
description: Implement closed-loop two-plane laser-ablation balancing with in-line spin measurement, replacing iterative mill-and-remeasure balancing of the IPM rotor assembly. First-pass yield rises from approximately 85% to 97% to G2.5 grade at 16,000 rpm, cutting rotor scrap, cycle time, and rebalancing labor by about 3.50 euro per unit. Removes added balance weights entirely and shortens the line. Benchmarked against Li Auto 5C rear-drive rotor balancing line.

title: Direct oil-spray rotor cooling enabling magnet downgrade
description: Add a hollow-shaft direct oil-cooling circuit spraying ATF onto rotor end-rings, lowering peak magnet temperature ~25°C. The cooler operating point allows a one-grade magnet downgrade (N48UH to N45SH) and ~8% less Dy, saving ~€5/unit on magnets while sustaining continuous power. Oil path machined into existing shaft. Benchmarked against AITO M9 oil-cooled drive motor.

title: Integrated cast water jacket replacing welded steel cooling shell
description: Cast the spiral water jacket directly into the HPDC AlSi10MnMg motor housing instead of a separate rolled-and-welded steel cooling shroud with O-ring seals. Eliminates the shroud part, two weld seams, and a leak-test station, removing ~0.6 kg and ~€8/unit. Coolant channel cross-section optimized via core pins for 12% lower pressure drop. Benchmarked against Mercedes eATS integrated stator jacket.

title: Combined stator-jacket and rotor oil loop sharing one pump
description: Merge water-glycol stator jacket and ATF rotor-spray cooling into a single oil-only loop using a shared electric oil pump and one heat exchanger, deleting the secondary coolant pump and water/oil interface seals. Removes ~5 fittings and one pump (~€12/unit) and simplifies fill/bleed. Direct end-winding oil contact improves thermal headroom for continuous power. Benchmarked against Tesla-style/BYD direct-oil EDU thermal design.

title: Bare-die SiC MOSFET power module replacing IGBT in inverter
description: Replace 750V Si IGBT modules with 1200V SiC MOSFET half-bridge modules for the 800V inverter, cutting switching loss ~70% and conduction loss at partial load. Higher efficiency permits ~5% smaller battery for equal range and shrinks the heatsink and DC-link cap, a net vehicle-level saving despite die premium, ~€18/unit system. Enables 800V fast charge. Benchmarked against Porsche Taycan/PPE SiC inverter.

title: Sintered-silver die-attach replacing solder in SiC power module
description: Use pressure-assisted silver sintering for SiC die-attach instead of high-Pb solder, raising thermal cycling life ~5x and allowing junction operation to 175°C. The improved thermal path lets the module use ~12% less SiC die area for the same continuous current, offsetting silver cost for ~€4/unit net plus warranty reduction. Benchmarked against AVATR/CATL 800V SiC module.

title: Film DC-link capacitor downsizing via reduced switching ripple
description: Raise SiC switching frequency to 16 kHz with interleaved control to cut DC-link RMS ripple current ~30%, allowing the polypropylene film DC-link capacitor to shrink from 500 µF to 350 µF. Removes ~0.25 L of capacitor volume and ~€7/unit while easing busbar layout. Thermal margin on the cap improves at 800V. Benchmarked against Denza/BYD 800V e-axle inverter.

title: Integrated PCB gate driver replacing discrete driver board
description: Integrate isolated gate-driver ICs directly onto the main control PCB with planar pulse transformers, deleting the separate driver daughterboard, its connector, and harness. Removes ~14 components and one board (~€5/unit), shortens gate loop inductance for cleaner SiC switching, and cuts assembly steps. Desat protection retained on-die. Benchmarked against Luxeed S7 800V inverter control stack.

title: Laminated busbar replacing cable links between module and caps
description: Replace insulated copper cable links and ring-terminal bolting between power module, DC-link cap, and HV input with a stamped laminated copper busbar. Cuts loop inductance ~40% to reduce SiC overshoot, removes 6 crimped terminals and 4 fasteners (~€4/unit), and lowers I²R loss. One press-formed part replaces a sub-assembly. Benchmarked against Maextro/Hongqi 800V inverter busbar.

title: Pin-fin direct-cooled SiC baseplate eliminating TIM layer
description: Adopt a pin-fin AlSiC baseplate directly immersed in the coolant channel, deleting the thermal interface grease layer and a separate cold plate. Thermal resistance junction-to-coolant drops ~35%, allowing ~10% smaller SiC die for equal current and removing the TIM dispense station, ~€6/unit. Sealing via single perimeter gasket. Benchmarked against Audi PPE 800V direct-cooled inverter.

title: Net-shape forged helical gears reducing machining stock
description: Switch reduction gears to precision warm-forged near-net blanks, leaving only finish grinding stock. Removes rough-hobbing and turning operations, cutting ~0.4 kg machining swarf per gearset and ~2 minutes cycle time. Material utilization rises from ~60% to ~85% on 20MnCr5, saving ~€6/unit plus tool wear. Tooth root strength improved by forged grain flow. Benchmarked against Mercedes eATS two-stage reduction.

title: Honed gear flanks replacing hard-grinding for NVH targets
description: Replace generating grinding of helical reduction gears with gear honing, achieving the surface finish and flank modification needed for 17,000 rpm NVH at lower cycle time and tool cost. Honing removes the grinding wheel consumable and cuts ~1.4 min/part, ~€4.50/unit, while the cross-hatch finish lowers mesh whine ~2 dB. Benchmarked against Zeekr 800V single-speed reducer.

title: Lower-alloy steel gear with optimized case-carburizing
description: Substitute 27MnCr5 for premium 18CrNiMo7-6 on the intermediate gear, paired with low-pressure vacuum carburizing and high-pressure gas quench to hit the same case depth and core hardness. Reduces alloy cost ~€3.20/unit and distortion (cutting grind stock), with bending fatigue validated to the 800V torque spectrum. Benchmarked against Xpeng rear single-speed gearbox.

title: Single-piece differential housing replacing bolted two-half case
description: Replace a two-piece bolted differential carrier with a single forged carrier using a pressed-in welded ring gear, deleting 8 bolts, the joint face machining, and a sealing interface. Removes ~0.5 kg and ~€5/unit plus one leak path. Laser-welded ring gear cuts assembly time. Validated for the EDU peak axle torque. Benchmarked against BYD e-Platform 3.0 differential.

title: Hybrid passive-active lubrication deleting full oil pump
description: Adopt a hybrid lubrication scheme where churning/splash from the ring gear supplies gear-mesh oil and a small electric pump only feeds bearings and rotor cooling, allowing a downsized 6W pump instead of a 25W gerotor unit. Removes ~half the pump cost (~€8/unit) and one pressure regulator. Oil galleries cast into housing. Benchmarked against Li Auto 5C EDU lubrication.

title: Plastic gerotor oil-pump rotor set replacing sintered steel
description: Replace sintered-steel gerotor inner/outer rotors with glass-filled PPS injection-molded rotors rated for ATF at 130°C. Cuts pump rotor cost ~€2.40/unit, reduces mass and inertia for faster prime, and removes a machining/sizing step. Clearances held by precision molding. Validated for 800V EDU oil flow at 16,000 rpm pump speed. Benchmarked against Nio 2nd-gen EDU oil pump.

title: Shared single oil for gearbox and motor cooling deleting filter
description: Use one low-viscosity ATF for both gear lubrication and direct motor oil-cooling with a single sump and magnetic drain plug, deleting the secondary fluid, a barrier seal, and one spin-on filter in favor of an integrated screen. Removes ~€4/unit and simplifies service fill. Lower-viscosity oil also cuts churning loss ~1.5%. Benchmarked against AITO M9 dual-motor EDU.

title: Cast-in oil-to-coolant heat exchanger in EDU housing
description: Integrate the oil-to-water heat exchanger as cast channels in the HPDC EDU housing rather than a bolted-on stacked-plate cooler, removing the cooler part, its 2 seals, 4 bolts, and 2 oil hoses. Saves ~€9/unit and ~0.7 kg, shortens oil path for lower pressure drop, and eliminates an external leak interface. Benchmarked against Mercedes integrated eATS thermal block.

title: Thermistor-in-busbar deleting standalone coolant temp sensor
description: Embed an NTC thermistor into the inverter coolant inlet boss and infer winding temperature via a thermal model, deleting a separate threaded coolant temperature sensor and its connector/harness branch. Removes ~€2/unit and one leak-prone fitting while retaining derating accuracy within ±3°C. Software model calibrated on dyno. Benchmarked against Volvo EX90 EDU thermal control.

title: Single 800V coolant loop merging inverter and motor branches
description: Combine separate inverter and motor coolant branches into one series 800V EDU loop with a single inlet/outlet pair, deleting one Y-splitter, 2 hoses, and 4 quick-connects at the e-axle. Saves ~€7/unit and reduces fill/bleed complexity; flow split tuned by internal orifice. Lowers overall coolant volume ~0.4 L. Benchmarked against Porsche/PPE integrated drive cooling.

title: One-piece HV interface module consolidating three-phase terminals
description: Replace three individual phase connectors between inverter and motor with a single integrated three-phase busbar terminal block sealed by one gasket, deleting 2 connector bodies, 6 ring terminals, and 3 bolts. Removes ~€6/unit, cuts a leak interface, and lowers contact resistance. Direct laser-welded phase joints inside the housing. Benchmarked against BMW Gen6 800V integrated HV interface.

title: Aluminum HV busbars with selective plating replacing copper
description: Switch internal HV power busbars from copper to 6101-T6 aluminum with selective tin/silver plating only at bolted joints. For the 800V/400A path the cross-section grows ~30% but mass drops ~50% and material cost ~60%, netting ~€8/unit. Joint creep mitigated by Belleville washers. Validated for fast-charge current. Benchmarked against BYD blade-platform HV distribution.

title: Spring-contact HVIL replacing dedicated interlock loop wiring
description: Integrate the high-voltage interlock (HVIL) into the main HV connector with short-pin spring contacts instead of a separate low-voltage interlock harness routed to each HV joint. Deletes ~3 LV connectors and ~1.2 m of wire (~€3/unit) while keeping fail-safe disconnect detection. Simplifies service de-energization. Benchmarked against Audi PPE 800V connector system.

title: Inductive position sensor replacing resolver on EDU
description: Replace the wound resolver and its rotor-stator transformer with a PCB-based inductive position sensor, deleting the resolver winding assembly, its precision bracket, and the resolver-to-digital converter. Cuts ~€9/unit and ~0.15 kg, with comparable accuracy for FOC at 18,000 rpm and easier 800V EMC. Mounts on existing end-cap PCB. Benchmarked against Zeekr/CATL e-axle inductive sensing.

title: Coreless Hall current sensing replacing closed-loop fluxgate
description: Replace closed-loop fluxgate phase current sensors with PCB-integrated coreless Hall sensors measuring busbar field, deleting the magnetic core, secondary winding, and sensor module housing. Saves ~€4/unit across two sensed phases and reduces inverter volume. Bandwidth and accuracy meet 16 kHz SiC FOC needs with on-die temperature compensation. Benchmarked against Xpeng 800V inverter current sensing.

title: Sensorless high-speed control reducing redundant position sensor
description: Deploy flux-observer sensorless control above 1,500 rpm with the inductive sensor used only at low speed, eliminating a redundant backup position sensor and its harness previously fitted for ASIL-D fallback. Removes ~€3/unit and one connector while meeting functional-safety via model redundancy. Improves field-weakening robustness at 800V. Benchmarked against Nio second-gen drive control.

title: Single-MCU consolidation of motor and DCDC control
description: Consolidate separate motor-control and auxiliary DC-DC controller MCUs onto a single automotive multi-core SoC with hardware partitioning, deleting one MCU, its crystal, power supply, and CAN transceiver. Removes ~14 components and ~€7/unit while cutting inter-controller latency. Retains ASIL-D for the motor lockstep core. Benchmarked against Hongqi 800V integrated power controller.

title: 3-in-1 EDU integrating motor, inverter and reducer housing
description: Integrate motor, SiC inverter, and single-speed reducer into one shared HPDC AlSi10MnMg housing, deleting separate inverter and gearbox cases, their mating flanges, ~30 fasteners, 3 external HV cables, and 4 coolant hoses. Saves ~€35/unit and ~4 kg, shortens phase busbars, and removes two leak interfaces. Benchmarked against BYD 8-in-1/3-in-1 e-axle.

title: High-vacuum HPDC EDU case enabling structural weld repairs delete
description: Move the EDU housing to high-vacuum die casting with optimized gating to cut gas porosity, allowing T5 heat-treat and reliable leak performance without impregnation sealing. Eliminates the vacuum-impregnation step and lowers scrap from porosity rejects from ~6% to ~1.5%, saving ~€7/unit. Enables thinner ribbed walls for ~0.5 kg mass cut. Benchmarked against Maextro/Hongqi cast e-axle housing.

title: Press-fit bearing bores deleting separate bearing carriers
description: Machine rotor and intermediate-shaft bearing seats directly into the cast EDU housing instead of using separate steel bearing carrier inserts, deleting 2 carrier parts, 8 bolts, and 2 alignment dowels. Saves ~€6/unit and one stack-up tolerance, improving shaft alignment for NVH. Requires precision line-boring fixture. Benchmarked against Mercedes integrated eATS housing.

title: Hairpin laser-weld inline CT inspection cutting end-of-line scrap
description: Add inline optical/thermal weld inspection of all hairpin joints right after laser welding instead of catching defects at end-of-line hi-pot, so a single bad weld is reworked early rather than scrapping a finished stator worth ~€140. First-pass quality detection raises yield ~2.5 points, saving ~€3.50/unit net of the camera cell. Benchmarked against Audi PPE hairpin welding line.

title: Die-cast copper rotor cage option for induction auxiliary motor
description: For the front induction (ASM) motor of a dual-motor 800V AWD, adopt a die-cast copper rotor cage instead of aluminum, raising rotor conductivity ~40% to cut rotor I²R loss and remove all rare-earth magnets entirely. The magnet-free rotor saves ~€15/unit versus an IPM front motor and improves drag-free coasting efficiency. Benchmarked against Audi e-tron/PPE front ASM.

title: Reduced-magnet-count spoke rotor using ferrite-assist design
description: Adopt a hybrid spoke rotor combining a reduced NdFeB content with flux-concentrating ferrite segments, cutting heavy/light rare-earth magnet mass ~25% for the same airgap flux. Saves ~€10/unit on a 2.2 kg magnet set while ferrite adds negligible cost. Spoke geometry raises torque density and tolerates higher temperature. Benchmarked against Denza/BYD low-rare-earth motor program.

title: Hybrid ceramic-steel bearing only on high-speed rotor end
description: Use a hybrid ceramic-ball bearing solely at the high-speed rotor drive end to handle 18,000 rpm and electrical-discharge risk, while retaining standard steel bearings elsewhere, rather than fitting premium bearings throughout. Targeted use removes the need for a separate shaft grounding ring and cuts bearing-current EDM failures, net ~€3/unit including warranty. Benchmarked against Porsche Taycan high-speed drive bearings.

title: Full 8-in-1 800V power-electronics integration at system level
description: Integrate motor, SiC inverter, reducer, OBC, DC-DC, PDU, BMS interface, and VCU control into one 8-in-1 800V e-axle sharing housing, coolant loop, and HV busbars. Versus discrete boxes this deletes ~6 enclosures, ~12 HV connectors, and ~8 coolant joints, saving ~€60/vehicle and ~12 kg while cutting harness length. Common 800V DC backbone simplifies fast charge. Benchmarked against BYD 8-in-1 e-axle.

title: Boost-charge via motor windings deleting standalone 800V booster
description: Reuse the drive motor windings and inverter as a boost converter to charge an 800V pack from a 400V DC fast charger, deleting the dedicated standalone DC-DC boost module, its magnetics, and cooling. Removes a ~€45/vehicle assembly and ~5 kg while retaining 400V-station compatibility. Requires firmware and contactor reconfiguration only. Benchmarked against Xpeng/Hyundai-style 800V boost charging.

title: Shared 800V coolant and HV architecture across front/rear EDUs
description: Standardize one 800V HV-distribution and coolant architecture shared by front and rear EDUs using common connectors, busbar pitch, and a single series coolant manifold, removing variant-specific harnesses and duplicate PDU branches. Cuts ~€20/vehicle and reduces part numbers ~30% across the AWD platform. Eases plant complexity and spares. Benchmarked against Zeekr 800V AWD platform.

title: Platform inverter scaling via die-count instead of unique designs
description: Scale inverter power across the lineup by varying SiC die count on a common power-module footprint and shared gate driver/control PCB, rather than designing unique inverters per motor. Reuse of busbars, caps, and housing across 3 power classes cuts NRE and raises volume per part number, lowering unit cost ~€12 via economies of scale. Benchmarked against Mercedes modular inverter family.

title: Common EDU platform with module-count motor length scaling
description: Define one EDU family where torque scales by stator stack length and SiC die count on shared housing, reducer, and bearing set, eliminating bespoke EDUs per model. Carry-over of ~70% of parts across power variants raises volume and cuts tooling amortization, lowering EDU unit cost ~€18. Simplifies validation and plant changeover. Benchmarked against BMW Gen6 modular eDrive family.

title: Magnet-free externally-excited rotor EDU eliminating rare earth
description: Adopt an externally-excited synchronous motor (EESM) using a wound rotor with inductive (brushless) excitation, removing all NdFeB magnets and Dy/Tb from the EDU. Eliminates ~€30/unit of rare-earth magnets and supply-chain risk, improves high-speed efficiency via field control, and cuts spin loss when coasting. Adds rotor winding and exciter offset by magnet delete. Benchmarked against BMW Gen5 EESM eDrive.

title: AA6082-T6 forged steering knuckle replacing GJS-450 cast iron
description: Switch the front upright from GJS-450 ductile-iron casting to a closed-die AA6082-T6 aluminium forging. Removes 2.1kg/corner (8.4kg/vehicle), cuts the machining envelope by 30% and eliminates two casting cores. Net saving roughly €11/corner from reduced unsprung mass enabling smaller brakes and lighter wheel bearings. Forging tooling amortises over 280k units. Benchmarks BMW G60 5-Series aluminium knuckle architecture.

title: 980MPa dual-phase steel stamped lower control arm
description: Replace forged-steel front lower control arm with a two-shell 980MPa DP stamped-and-welded design. Down-gauges wall from 4.2mm to 2.8mm, saving 1.3kg/arm while meeting 35kN buckling. Stamping replaces forging plus six machining ops, dropping piece cost about €7/corner and halving cycle time. Higher yield strength removes a heat-treat step. Benchmarks Mercedes EQE rear multilink stamped arm strategy.

title: Hollow tube stabiliser bar replacing solid forged bar
description: Convert solid 26mm front anti-roll bar to a DOM seamless hollow tube (28mm OD, 4mm wall) with the same roll stiffness. Mass drops from 4.6kg to 2.7kg, a 41% reduction, while bending fatigue is maintained via induction-hardened ends. Saves about €5.50/vehicle in raw steel and reduces forging energy. Benchmarks Audi Q5 hollow stabiliser bar adoption.

title: GFRP composite transverse leaf spring rear axle module
description: Replace two rear coil springs plus lower links with a single glass-fibre composite transverse leaf spring that also reacts lateral loads. Deletes two coils, two spring seats and one link per axle, saving 4.8kg/vehicle and removing eight assembly fasteners. System cost falls roughly €18/vehicle at 150k volume despite higher material price. Benchmarks Volvo SPA transverse composite leaf spring.

title: Flow-formed aluminium wheel replacing low-pressure cast
description: Move 19-inch wheels from gravity low-pressure casting to flow-formed (spin-forged) rim barrels. Grain refinement allows 1.4kg/wheel reduction (5.6kg/set) at equal radial fatigue, and the thinner rim cuts aluminium input 12%. Net saving about €9/wheel from material and improved scrap yield, offsetting the flow-forming machine. Benchmarks Zeekr 001 flow-formed wheel program.

title: Single-piece cast aluminium rear subframe vs welded steel
description: Replace 14-piece welded steel rear subframe with a single AlSi10MnMg high-pressure die-cast unit. Consolidates 14 stampings and 38 weld joints into one casting, removing the entire weld cell and saving 6.5kg/vehicle. Tooling is high but piece cost drops about €22/vehicle and takt time falls 40s. Benchmarks Tesla-style megacasting echoed in Xpeng G6 rear underbody.

title: Friction-welded tubular half-shaft replacing solid bar
description: Replace solid forged half-shaft bar with a friction-welded tubular shaft joining two splined stub ends to a thin-wall tube. Torsional capacity of 3200Nm is retained while mass drops 1.1kg/side and rotating inertia falls 22%. Friction welding removes a forging operation and reduces steel input, saving about €6/side. Benchmarks BYD e-axle tubular half-shaft.

title: Integrated wheel-bearing hub (Gen3) replacing Gen1 plus knuckle press
description: Adopt a Gen3 flanged hub-bearing unit that bolts directly to the upright, deleting the press-fit Gen1 bearing, separate hub and two snap rings. Removes the press station and one balancing op, cutting warranty NVH claims. Assembly time falls 25s/corner and piece cost about €4/corner net. Pre-greased sealed-for-life design. Benchmarks Porsche Macan EV Gen3 hub.

title: Carbon-ceramic-free: cast-iron disc with laser-clad coating
description: Apply a laser-clad tungsten-carbide friction layer to a grey-iron GG20 disc, eliminating the carbon-ceramic option while meeting EU7 brake-dust limits. Wear and corrosion drop 90% versus bare iron, deleting zinc plating and extending service to 150k km. Adds €30/disc cladding but avoids €280 ceramic premium, saving roughly €40/corner on equipped trims. Benchmarks Mercedes-Porsche surface-coated disc.

title: Air suspension to FSD frequency-selective passive damper system
description: Replace four-corner air spring plus ECAS compressor with steel coils and frequency-selective passive dampers on mid trims. Deletes compressor, reservoir, four air struts, ride-height sensors and valve block, removing 14 components and 9kg/vehicle. System cost falls about €120/vehicle while retaining 80% of comfort metric. Benchmarks Li Auto trim-walk to passive coils on entry variants.

title: Disconnect coupling deleting rear e-axle drag at cruise
description: Add a dog-clutch disconnect to the rear secondary e-axle that decouples reduction gears and half-shafts during single-motor cruise, cutting spin losses by 0.9kW. The 35Wh/100km saving lets battery cells shrink 1.2kWh at equal range, a system-level material saving of about €70/vehicle that dwarfs the €18 actuator. Benchmarks AVATR dual-motor disconnect e-axle.

title: GJS-500 ductile-iron caliper bracket replacing forged steel
description: Switch the floating-caliper anchor bracket from forged C45 steel to GJS-500-7 ductile iron casting. Casting near-net shape removes three milling ops and the forging die, while 500MPa tensile meets 28kN clamp reaction. Saves about €3.20/corner and improves dimensional repeatability. Slight 180g mass gain accepted at the unsprung rear. Benchmarks Denza rear caliper bracket sourcing.

title: Hydroformed front subframe rails replacing welded box sections
description: Hydroform the front subframe side rails as closed tubes, replacing two-shell stamped-and-welded box sections. Eliminates 22 spot welds and one sealing operation per rail, improving torsional stiffness 18% at equal mass. Process consolidation cuts about €14/vehicle and removes a weld fixture. Benchmarks Audi space-frame hydroformed node experience applied to subframe.

title: Bonded micro-cellular PU jounce bumper replacing molded rubber top mount
description: Replace the molded-rubber damper top mount with a tunable micro-cellular polyurethane (Cellasto-type) bonded unit. Single-component design deletes the steel cup, snubber and washer stack, cutting four parts to one and saving 0.3kg/corner. Piece cost falls about €2.10/corner with better high-frequency isolation. Benchmarks BMW micro-cellular top-mount strategy.

title: Plunging tripod inboard CV joint replacing Rzeppa double-offset
description: Adopt a GI-type tripod plunging joint inboard instead of a double-offset Rzeppa ball joint. Three rollers and a tulip replace six balls, a cage and a wire ring, cutting part count and grinding operations. Lower friction trims 12W drag and saves about €5.50/side. Suited to 2800Nm e-axle torque with ±26mm plunge. Benchmarks Nio inboard tripod half-shaft.

title: Steel-backed aluminium hybrid brake disc replacing full cast iron
description: Use an aluminium hat riveted to a thin grey-iron friction ring (composite floating disc) instead of monobloc cast iron. Removes 2.4kg/corner unsprung mass and reduces coning. Aluminium hat is machined not cast-iron, cutting one foundry pour. Net saving about €6/corner after rivet assembly, with reduced thermal judder warranty. Benchmarks Porsche composite floating disc.

title: Integrated e-corner module consolidating hub motor, brake and steering
description: Package an in-wheel hub motor, by-wire brake and steer-by-wire actuator into one e-corner module, deleting half-shafts, CV joints, steering rack and column tubes vehicle-wide. Removes 11 driveline parts and the entire steering linkage, saving 14kg/vehicle and a full assembly station. System saving about €180/vehicle at scale. Benchmarks Yangwang U9 distributed e-corner architecture.

title: Tailor-rolled blank shock tower replacing multi-thickness welded stack
description: Form the front strut tower from a tailor-rolled blank with continuous 1.2–2.4mm thickness, replacing a three-piece reinforced weld stack. Deletes two reinforcement patches and 16 spot welds per side, saving 1.6kg/vehicle. Single-stamping eliminates a sub-assembly fixture, cutting about €8/vehicle. Benchmarks Mercedes tailor-rolled body-in-white reinforcements.

title: Plastic-composite rack housing replacing aluminium die-cast steering rack
description: Overmold a glass-reinforced PA66-GF50 housing around the steering rack tube instead of an AlSi9Cu3 die-casting. Mass falls 1.1kg and the casting plus its machining of mounting bosses is eliminated, molded-in instead. Corrosion sealing and NVH damping improve. Net saving about €9/vehicle on a belt-drive EPS rack. Benchmarks Audi composite steering housing studies.

title: Single-stage helical reduction replacing two-stage e-axle gearset
description: Redesign the secondary e-axle to a single helical reduction stage with a higher-speed 18000rpm motor, deleting one gear pair, an intermediate shaft and two bearings. Removes four ground gear flanks and one shaft grinding op, cutting about €16/axle and 2.3kg. Efficiency holds at 96% in the cruise window. Benchmarks Zeekr single-stage rear drive unit.

title: Press-fit ball joint replacing bolted three-piece tapered joint
description: Integrate the lower ball joint as a press-fit cartridge into the control arm rather than a separate bolted, tapered unit with castle nut and cotter pin. Deletes the bolt, nut and pin, removing a torque-and-pin station. Saves about €2.80/corner and 0.2kg. Maintainability handled by service replacement of the full arm. Benchmarks BYD Seal press-fit ball joint.

title: Hollow-bored differential pinion shaft replacing solid forging
description: Gun-drill the differential pinion shaft to a hollow core, removing 0.6kg rotating mass at equal 18CrNiMo7 case-hardened torsional capacity. Lower inertia improves NVH and efficiency marginally. Bore replaces no machining but reduces forging input 14%, saving about €3.40/axle. Benchmarks Hongqi rear differential hollow pinion.

title: Two-piece bolted propshaft replacing three-piece with center bearing
description: Shorten the AWD propshaft to a two-piece carbon-wrapped aluminium design, deleting the center support bearing, its bracket and one universal joint. Carbon overwrap raises the bending critical speed above 8500rpm, removing the intermediate support. Saves 2.1kg and about €13/vehicle. Benchmarks AITO M9 lightweight propshaft.

title: Cold-formed splined CV outer race replacing machined-from-bar
description: Net-shape cold-form the outer race ball tracks of the wheel-side fixed CV joint instead of turning and grinding from bar stock.
