Cold forming removes two grinding passes and improves surface hardness through work-hardening, lifting fatigue 15%. Material utilisation rises from 62% to 88%, saving about €4.20/side. Benchmarks Maextro front half-shaft joint sourcing.

title: Hydraulic-mount-free elastomer e-axle mount with tuned voided bushing
description: Replace the hydraulically damped e-axle torque-rod mount with a tuned voided elastomer bushing, since EV powertrain lacks combustion shake. Deletes the fluid chamber, diaphragm and decoupler, simplifying to a single molded part. Saves about €6/mount across three mounts (€18/vehicle) with equal NVH on the EV duty cycle. Benchmarks Luxeed S7 EV powertrain mounts.

title: Aluminium-MMC brake rotor replacing grey iron on light trims
description: Adopt an aluminium-SiC metal-matrix-composite rotor for low-mass EV trims with strong regen, where mechanical braking is light-duty. Cuts 3.0kg/corner unsprung mass and corrosion-driven warranty, deleting zinc coating. MMC premium offset by regen-reduced wear extending life to vehicle lifetime, net about €8/corner over the cycle. Benchmarks Nio ET5 lightweight rotor trials.

title: Multi-link rear consolidated into single forged H-arm
description: Replace a five-link rear suspension with an AA6082 forged H-arm integrating two lateral and one trailing function. Reduces links from five to two per side, deleting six bushings and six fasteners per corner. Mass falls 3.2kg/corner and assembly time 60s/axle. System saving about €26/vehicle. Benchmarks BMW integral-link consolidation direction.

title: Roll-formed spring seat replacing machined aluminium seat
description: Roll-form the lower coil-spring seat from coated steel sheet instead of machining it from an aluminium extrusion. Forming replaces turning and reduces material 40%, while a captive elastomer isolator is clipped not bonded. Saves about €2.40/corner with equal corrosion life via cathodic e-coat. Benchmarks Volvo roll-formed spring seat.

title: Steer-by-wire deleting steering column and intermediate shaft
description: Implement full steer-by-wire, deleting the steering column, two universal joints, the intermediate shaft and the firewall seal grommet. Replaces mechanical path with a feedback actuator and road-wheel actuator on the rack. Removes a crash-collapse column assembly, saving 3.5kg and about €40/vehicle net after redundant ECU. Benchmarks Maextro steer-by-wire deployment.

title: Bushing-integrated control arm with molded-in voided bushings
description: Vulcanise the rubber bushings directly into the aluminium control-arm eyes rather than press-fitting separate steel-sleeve bushings. Deletes the outer sleeve and the press operation, and removes interference-fit scrap. Saves about €3.60/corner and one press station. Tuned voids give axial-radial rate split. Benchmarks Mercedes integrated-bushing arm.

title: Single-piece die-cast front shock tower plus longitudinal node
description: Die-cast the front shock tower integrated with the longitudinal rail front node in AlSi10MnMg, replacing 9 stamped parts and 46 spot welds. Consolidation removes a weld sub-cell and saves 5.4kg/vehicle. Casting tooling amortises over 200k units, yielding about €19/vehicle. Improves NVH path stiffness 22%. Benchmarks Xpeng front megacast node.

title: Plastic composite coil spring replacing steel coil
description: Adopt a glass-fibre-reinforced polymer coil spring replacing the chrome-silicon steel coil. Cuts 1.9kg/corner unsprung mass and eliminates shot-peening, e-coat and chip protection entirely, removing three finishing ops. Corrosion-proof for life. Material premium offset by deleted coatings, net about €4/corner. Benchmarks Audi GFRP coil spring program.

title: Welded-on disconnect dog clutch eliminating front e-axle gearbox losses
description: Integrate a low-mass dog-clutch disconnect on the front primary e-axle so the motor and gear train fully decouple during rear-drive cruise. Saves 1.1kW of churning and bearing drag, equating to 45Wh/100km. Range gain permits 1.5kWh smaller pack, a net system saving near €80/vehicle versus the €20 clutch. Benchmarks Denza front-axle disconnect.

title: Cast GJS-600 lower control arm replacing forged aluminium on heavy trims
description: On heavy 2.6t SUV trims, switch the forged-aluminium lower arm to a GJS-600-3 ductile-iron casting where unsprung-mass sensitivity is lower. Near-net casting deletes forging plus five machining ops, and iron is far cheaper per kg. Saves about €7/corner despite a 1.4kg mass gain accepted on the platform. Benchmarks Hongqi heavy-SUV iron arm.

title: Box-section welded subframe replacing cast aluminium on volume trim
description: For the high-volume base trim, replace the premium cast-aluminium front subframe with a 590MPa steel box-section welded design. Steel piece cost is about €34 lower than the aluminium casting despite 4kg gain, acceptable on FWD base cars. Welded build reuses existing body-shop robots, avoiding casting capex. Benchmarks BYD Dolphin steel subframe.

title: Sealed-for-life electric park brake caliper deleting drum-in-hat
description: Replace the rear drum-in-hat park brake plus cable with an integrated electric-park-brake motor-on-caliper. Deletes the drum, shoes, cable, lever and adjuster, removing eight parts and one assembly station. Saves about €11/corner and 1.3kg. Self-adjusting, no cable routing. Benchmarks Volvo EPB motor-on-caliper rear corner.

title: Induction-hardened raceway hub deleting separate inner ring
description: Induction-harden the bearing raceway directly onto the hub flange of the Gen3 unit, deleting one pressed inner ring and its grinding. Integrated raceway removes a component and a press-and-stake op, saving about €3.10/corner. Hardness profile reaches 58HRC at 0.9mm depth. Benchmarks Porsche integrated-raceway hub.

title: Coaxial e-axle integrating motor, gearbox and differential housing
description: Adopt a coaxial e-axle where the rotor shaft passes through one half-shaft, integrating motor, two-stage reduction and differential into a single die-cast housing. Deletes one housing joint, two seals and the offset bracket, saving 4.5kg and about €30/axle. Shared oil sump cuts a fill operation. Benchmarks AVATR coaxial drive unit.

title: Forged 7075-T6 monoblock caliper replacing two-piece cast
description: Replace the bolted two-piece cast-aluminium front caliper with a forged 7075-T6 monoblock. Forging deletes the bridge bolts and the split-face machining, raising stiffness 28% for better pedal feel and pad wear. Net about €5/corner after forging tooling, plus 0.4kg saved. Benchmarks Porsche monoblock front caliper.

title: Polymer composite leaf-spring trailing arm replacing steel link
description: Mold the rear trailing arm as a glass-fibre composite leaf integrating spring compliance and longitudinal location. Replaces a stamped-steel arm plus a separate bushing pair, deleting two press-fit bushings. Saves 1.7kg/corner and about €4.80/corner with corrosion-free life. Benchmarks Zeekr composite trailing arm.

title: Hollow stabiliser-bar link with molded ball-stud sockets
description: Replace the solid steel drop link with a hollow tube link and injection-molded POM ball-stud sockets, deleting metal socket cups and grease boots staking. Saves 0.4kg/corner and about €2.20/corner from material and assembly. Maintenance-free sealed sockets. Benchmarks AITO stabiliser drop-link sourcing.

title: Lubricated-for-life sealed propshaft U-joint replacing greaseable
description: Switch the propshaft universal joints to sealed lubricated-for-life cross-and-bearing units, deleting the grease zerk and the service relube operation. Improved seal package extends life beyond 240k km. Saves about €1.80/joint across four joints and removes a service interval. Benchmarks Li Auto sealed U-joint.

title: Plasma-transferred-arc clad valve seats deleting hardened insert subframe bush sleeves
description: On the subframe bushing interfaces, replace pressed-in hardened steel sleeves with a laser-clad wear track on the aluminium subframe bore. Cladding removes the sleeve component and its press-fit op while resisting fretting at the bush. Saves about €3/vehicle across four bushes and one station. Benchmarks Audi subframe bore cladding.

title: Single planetary reduction replacing offset two-shaft on rear drive
description: Replace the offset two-shaft helical reduction with a coaxial single planetary gearset on the rear drive unit. Balanced planet loads cut bearing count from six to four and reduce housing size, saving 3.1kg and about €15/axle. Mesh efficiency improves 0.4% lowering loss-driven cooling. Benchmarks Yangwang planetary rear unit.

title: Aluminium knuckle with cast-in steel bearing seat bimetal
description: Cast the aluminium upright around a steel bearing-seat insert (bimetal HPDC), deleting a pressed steel sleeve and its insertion op while giving a robust 58HRC bore for the hub bearing. Saves 1.5kg vs iron knuckle and about €4.50/corner over insert-press route. Benchmarks Luxeed bimetal knuckle.

title: Integrated brake-by-wire system deleting vacuum booster and lines
description: Adopt a one-box electro-hydraulic brake-by-wire unit, deleting the vacuum booster, vacuum pump, separate ESC modulator and several brake lines. Consolidates three modules into one, saving 3.8kg and about €45/vehicle while improving regen blending. Removes a vacuum-line assembly station. Benchmarks Nio one-box brake-by-wire.

title: Flow-formed half-shaft tube replacing thick-wall machined tube
description: Flow-form the half-shaft interconnecting tube to a variable wall (3.0mm ends, 2.2mm mid), replacing a constant thick-wall machined tube. Grain alignment lets mass drop 0.7kg/side at equal 3000Nm capacity, and flow-forming removes an internal turning op. Saves about €3.60/side. Benchmarks BYD flow-formed shaft tube.

title: Modular front corner module pre-assembled off-line
description: Pre-assemble the full front corner (knuckle, hub, brake, damper, arms, link) as a modular unit fitted to the subframe in one bolted operation on the main line. Moves 14 sequential line tasks off-line, cutting main-line takt 90s/vehicle and rework. Logistics and station savings net about €24/vehicle. Benchmarks Mercedes corner-module assembly.

title: Lightweight aluminium air-spring piston replacing steel rolling lobe
description: Replace the steel air-spring rolling-lobe piston with an impact-extruded aluminium piston and a deep-drawn aluminium top cap. Cuts 0.9kg/corner and deletes a phosphate-and-paint corrosion line, saving about €5/corner. Extrusion gives the contour in one stroke. Benchmarks BMW air-spring aluminium piston.

title: Full air suspension system consolidating compressor and ECU one-box
description: Consolidate the air-supply unit, valve block, dryer, reservoir and air-suspension ECU into one integrated module, deleting external air lines, two brackets and a separate controller housing. Reduces fittings from 12 to 5 and a leak-test station, saving about €38/vehicle and 2.6kg. Benchmarks Li Auto integrated air-supply module.

title: Mono-material PET instrument panel substrate for recyclability
description: Replace multi-material PA6-GF/PUR/PVC IP build-up with a mono-material PET substrate plus PET-foam skin, enabling closed-loop recycling and eliminating material separation at end-of-life. Single-resin sourcing cuts compound cost €2.10/vehicle and removes the adhesion-promoter coating step. Recyclate content rises to 30% without performance loss. Validated against Volvo EX90 mono-material interior strategy targeting full IP recyclability.

title: PP-LGF30 cross-car beam replacing magnesium die-casting
description: Substitute the magnesium die-cast cross-car beam with a hybrid PP-LGF30 (long-glass-fibre) injection-moulded beam with localised steel inserts for steering-column stiffness. Consolidates 14 brackets into the moulded geometry, removing 22 fasteners and 0.9kg. Avoids volatile magnesium pricing and high-pressure die-cast tooling, saving €11.40/vehicle. NVH targets met via tuned rib pattern. Benchmarked to BMW Neue Klasse polymer cockpit carrier.

title: In-mould-grain (IMG) door card eliminating paint and grain steps
description: Move the upper door trim to in-mould-grain technology, transferring the grain texture directly during compression moulding and removing the separate spray-paint and laser-etch grain steps. Eliminates two finishing stations and reduces VOC abatement load. Net saving €4.30/part across both front doors, with improved grain consistency. Benchmarked against Mercedes EQE door-card IMG implementation.

title: Foamed TPO skin replacing slush-moulded PVC for IP cover
description: Replace heavy slush-moulded PVC IP skin with a foamed TPO (thermoplastic olefin) skin produced by negative thermoforming. TPO is fully recyclable, 35% lighter, and free of plasticiser fogging, removing the fog-test rework loop. Material cost drops €3.60/vehicle and the slush tool's nickel shell maintenance disappears. Soft-touch haptics retained via 2.5mm PUR backfoam. Benchmarked to Audi Q6 e-tron TPO IP skin.

title: Single-shot 2K injection centre console with integrated trim
description: Produce the centre console base and soft cup-holder lip in a single-shot 2K injection process, combining ABS structure and TPE soft-touch in one cycle. Removes a separate over-moulded insert, two clips, and the manual assembly station. Parts consolidated from 5 to 2, saving €3.90/vehicle and 18 seconds cycle time. Benchmarked against Porsche Macan EV console module.

title: Natural-fibre (flax) door trim carrier replacing wood-fibre board
description: Adopt a flax-PP natural-fibre composite carrier for the door card, compression-moulded in one step versus the wood-fibre board plus PP backing. Cuts carrier weight 25% (0.6kg/door) and material cost €2.80/vehicle, while the renewable fibre lowers CO2 footprint for ESG scoring. Mouldability allows integrated map-pocket ribs, removing two fasteners. Benchmarked to BMW i-series natural-fibre door panels.

title: Bio-PU foam seat cushions from castor-oil polyol
description: Substitute up to 30% of petroleum polyol with castor-oil-derived bio-polyol in moulded seat-cushion bio-PU foam. Drop-in compatibility with existing foam-in-place tooling means zero capex. Stabilises foam cost against crude-linked feedstock swings, saving an estimated €2.20/seat, and supports recycled-content marketing. Comfort hardness (CLD) retained within spec. Benchmarked to Mercedes bio-based foam adoption in EQ-series seating.

title: Vegan microfibre seat trim replacing genuine leather
description: Replace genuine leather across the full seat-trim set with a recycled-PET vegan microfibre suede offering consistent yield and no hide-defect scrap. Cut-and-sew yield improves from 78% to 94%, removing tannery lead-time and slashing material cost €18/seat-set. The synthetic eliminates the chrome-tanning environmental burden and stabilises pricing against hide markets. Durability exceeds 60k Martindale cycles with retained soft haptics. Benchmarked to Polestar/Volvo Nordico vegan microfibre interior.

title: Integrated pillar-to-headliner one-piece moulded trim
description: Combine the A-pillar trim and headliner edge into a single low-pressure-moulded PUR/glass-mat part with an integrated airbag-deployment chute and energy-absorbing ribs. Consolidates 3 parts into 1, removing 4 clips and a separate foam ramp, saving €2.60/vehicle and reducing rattle interfaces that drive warranty squeak claims. The tear-seam is tuned for FMVSS-208 head-impact and curtain-airbag breakout. Benchmarked against Audi A6 e-tron integrated headliner trim module.

title: Compression-moulded carpet with integrated NVH backing
description: Replace tufted carpet plus a separate EVA heavy-layer with a single compression-moulded needle-punch carpet incorporating a recycled-fibre absorber backing in one stack. Combining floor covering and mass-spring damping in one moulding removes the heavy-layer cut-and-place station and its fixing tape. Saves €5.10/vehicle and 1.1kg while meeting absorption-alpha and transmission-loss targets across the powertrain band. Mono-fibre construction aids end-of-life recycling. Benchmarked to Nio ET5 integrated floor carpet system.

title: Foam-in-place armrest pad eliminating cut-foam and adhesive
description: Switch the centre-console armrest from a die-cut PUR slab bonded to the carrier to a foam-in-place process injecting PUR directly into the closed-trim cavity. Removes the foam-cutting operation, spray adhesive, and manual wrap, saving €1.90/part and improving haptic uniformity. Cycle automated on a single carousel. Benchmarked to Li Auto L7 armrest foam-in-place line.

title: Common HVAC vent register platform across model range
description: Standardise the centre and side HVAC vent register on a single carry-over barrel-and-blade module across three platforms, varying only the visible bezel. Cuts active tool count from 9 to 3 and consolidates spend, lowering piece cost €2.40/vehicle through volume aggregation. Common air-direction kinematics simplify validation. Benchmarked against BYD e-platform 3.0 shared register architecture.

title: Slim integrated display bezel replacing chrome-trimmed frame
description: Replace the discrete chrome-galvanised display frame with a black-panel cover-glass bonded directly to a single moulded bezel via optical bonding, achieving an edge-to-edge integrated display. Removes the galvanic chrome process and a separate trim ring, saving €4.60/vehicle and eliminating chrome-line environmental cost. Improves perceived quality. Benchmarked to Zeekr 001 integrated display surround.

title: Edge-lit ambient lighting via single moulded light-guide
description: Replace the door ambient strip's discrete LED-per-segment harness with a single end-fed PMMA light-guide edge-lit by two RGB LEDs, achieving uniform glow. Reduces LED count from 14 to 2 per door and shortens the wiring harness, saving €3.10/vehicle. Single connector eases assembly. Light uniformity within 15% across the run. Benchmarked to Xpeng G6 light-guide ambient system.

title: One-piece moulded seat-back panel replacing carpet-wrapped board
description: Substitute the carpet-wrapped seat-back board with a single textured-grain injection-moulded PP seat-back panel, removing the carpet lamination and edge-wrap operations. Integrated snap features eliminate 6 clips per row. Saves €2.70/seat and improves scuff resistance for the cargo-load surface. Grain matches lower trim. Benchmarked to Tesla-style moulded seat-back adopted by AITO M7.

title: Full cockpit module commonisation across LHD/RHD
description: Re-architect the full IP/cockpit module to a symmetric cross-car-beam and ducting layout enabling a single LHD/RHD carry-over substrate with mirrored cluster cut-outs only. Halves IP tooling investment and consolidates logistics, lowering amortised cost €9.80/vehicle. Common HVAC core and wiring break-points reduce variant validation. Benchmarked to Hongqi shared cockpit platform strategy.

title: Recliner mechanism commonisation to single supplier platform
description: Consolidate three recliner variants onto one continuous-disc recliner platform with a common torsion-bar and pinion interface across all front seats. Volume aggregation drops the recliner piece cost €3.80/seat and removes two separate validation programs plus their durability test fleets. The common backrest-pivot geometry simplifies the seat-frame weld jig and spares logistics. Latch strength and backset retention meet ECE-R17. Benchmarked to Denza seat recliner standardisation.

title: Tailored-blank steel seat frame replacing multi-gauge weldment
description: Replace the multi-piece, multi-gauge seat-back frame weldment with a laser-welded tailored-blank stamping that places sheet thickness only where load paths demand it. The approach reduces 9 stamped parts to 3, removing 12 spot welds and 0.8kg, and saving €7.20/seat in material and weld-cell time. Crash performance and head-restraint backset for ECE-R17 are retained through the graded gauge transition. Benchmarked to Mercedes EQS lightweight seat-frame architecture.

title: Snap-fit B-pillar trim eliminating screw fasteners
description: Redesign the B-pillar upper trim attachment from 4 screws plus belt-guide bezel to integrated metal-spring snap clips and a captive seatbelt-guide feature. Removes 4 fasteners and one bezel per side, cutting €2.10/vehicle and 14 seconds line time. Clip retention validated to 90N pull-off and buzz-squeak. Benchmarked to Volvo EX30 fastener-reduced pillar trim.

title: Open-pore real-wood veneer via direct-press lamination
description: Produce open-pore wood decor trim by direct-pressing the veneer onto the carrier with a single matt PUR seal coat, removing the conventional multi-coat high-gloss lacquer-and-sand process entirely. This eliminates 5 paint and sand steps plus the flatting line, saving €6.40/vehicle while delivering the on-trend natural matt finish customers prefer. VOC emissions and energy per part both fall sharply. Benchmarked to Audi natural open-pore wood inlays.

title: Mono-material PET full door-trim module for recycling
description: Re-engineer the complete door-trim module to a mono-material PET system: PET carrier, PET-felt backing, and PET textile cover, all weldable and recyclable as one stream. Eliminates material separation and the mixed-adhesive bonding, cutting €6.90/door at end-of-life processing and simplifying sourcing. Acoustic targets met by needled PET felt. Benchmarked to Volvo EX90 mono-material door panel.

title: Knit-to-shape 3D seat cover reducing cut-and-sew scrap
description: Adopt 3D flat-knit-to-shape seat covers that knit the cushion and bolster contour in one piece, eliminating panel cutting, sewing seams, and offcut scrap. Material utilisation rises to 96% and assembly labour drops 40%, saving €8.10/seat. Variable knit density tunes breathability and bolster firmness. Benchmarked to Mercedes knitted seat-cover concept in EQ interiors.

title: Polypropylene-foam headliner replacing fibreglass core
description: Replace the glass-fibre/PU headliner substrate with a recyclable PP-foam core laminated to a PET knit face, removing irritant glass-fibre handling on the line and enabling mono-stream recycling at end-of-life. The full headliner is compression-moulded in one step, saving €3.40/vehicle and 0.5kg while improving sag resistance under high summer cabin temperatures. Acoustic absorption is retained via the open-cell foam. Benchmarked to BYD Seal PP-foam headliner module.

title: Integrated grab-handle and coat-hook in headliner moulding
description: Mould the rear coat-hook boss and the grab-handle damper bracket directly into the headliner substrate, removing two separate stamped-steel brackets and four screws from the build. The living-hinge damped grab handle then clips in without tools at the trim station. Parts are consolidated from 6 to 2, saving €1.80/vehicle and cutting line time. Pull-load capability to 200N and damping feel are validated. Benchmarked to Li Auto L9 integrated headliner features.

title: Foamed-core steering wheel rim replacing cast-magnesium armature wrap
description: Switch the steering-wheel rim from leather-wrapped over PU-foamed magnesium to an in-mould foamed TPE rim over a steel-tube armature, removing the hand-wrapping and stitching labour. Eliminates leather cut-and-sew and saves €5.30/wheel while maintaining grip haptics. Integrated heating film co-moulded. Benchmarked to Xpeng P7 moulded steering-wheel rim.

title: Single-piece floor console storage tray replacing modular bins
description: Consolidate the modular cup-holder insert, phone-tray liner, and coin bin into a single soft-touch over-moulded storage tray. Removes two separate inserts and their locating clips, saving €1.60/vehicle and simplifying assembly to one drop-in part. Anti-slip TPE base co-moulded. Benchmarked to Zeekr 007 console storage integration.

title: Low-pressure-moulded IP substrate with integrated ducts
description: Produce the full IP substrate by low-pressure moulding with integrated demister and centre-vent ducts formed in the part, eliminating separately moulded-and-welded ABS ducts. Removes 4 duct parts, 6 vibration welds, and a sealing-foam tape, saving €7.60/vehicle. Lower clamp tonnage cuts press energy. Benchmarked to Audi Q6 e-tron integrated-duct IP.

title: Common door-pull bracket across front and rear doors
description: Standardise the metal door-pull reinforcement bracket to one symmetric part used in all four doors, replacing four unique handed brackets. Volume quadruples on a single tool, dropping piece cost €2.20/vehicle and removing three part numbers from logistics. Pull-load to 400N validated common. Benchmarked to BYD Dolphin door-pull commonisation.

title: Recycled-PET felt C-pillar trim replacing moulded ABS
description: Replace the moulded ABS C-pillar trim with a compression-moulded recycled-PET felt panel offering integrated acoustic absorption and a premium textile finish. Removes the paint/grain step and adds sound absorption, saving €2.90/vehicle and 0.3kg. 100% recycled fibre supports ESG content targets. Benchmarked to Polestar/Volvo PET-felt pillar trims.

title: Heat-staked vent register assembly removing screws
description: Replace screwed bezel-to-barrel assembly of the HVAC vent register with ultrasonic heat-staking, removing 3 self-tapping screws per register. Cuts fastener cost and 9 seconds of assembly per unit, saving €1.40/vehicle across four registers. Joint strength exceeds screw retention with no thread-strip risk. Benchmarked to AITO M9 heat-staked register module.

title: Full seat-structure commonisation between front rows
description: Architect the complete front seat structure—frame, recliner, rails, height-adjuster—as a single carry-over module for driver and passenger, varying only switch-pack trim. Halves structure validation and doubles weld-line volume, cutting €8.40/seat. Common rail pitch simplifies floor-pan interface. Crash to ECE-R17 validated once. Benchmarked to Maextro S800 shared seat-structure approach.

title: Magnetic-attach floor mats replacing grommet anchors
description: Replace twist-lock grommet floor-mat anchors moulded into the carpet with low-profile magnetic pads bonded to the floor pan, removing two grommet inserts per footwell and the carpet-pierce operation. Saves €1.30/vehicle and improves mat retention against pedal interference. Benchmarked to Nio ES6 magnetic floor-mat retention.

title: Co-moulded ambient light into door-trim carrier
description: Integrate the ambient light-guide directly into the door-trim carrier via 2K co-moulding of clear PMMA into the structural PP, removing the separate clipped-in light bar and its bracket. Parts consolidated 4 to 1, saving €3.20/door and eliminating a fit-gap interface. Single LED feed at the carrier edge. Benchmarked to Mercedes co-moulded ambient door trim.

title: Blow-moulded hollow cross-car beam in PA-GF
description: Replace the steel tubular cross-car beam with a gas-assist/blow-moulded hollow PA6-GF35 beam carrying integrated air ducts and harness channels. Consolidates the beam, two ducts, and eight clips, removing 1.4kg and 16 fasteners, saving €10.20/vehicle. Steering-column stiffness met via local rib and steel insert. Benchmarked to BMW polymer cross-car beam development.

title: Single-material PP seat-cushion carrier replacing wire-and-foam
description: Replace the steel-wire seat-cushion suspension plus separate foam pad with a moulded PP-LGF carrier and bonded thin foam, integrating bolster geometry. Removes the wire-frame bending operation and clip-on pad, saving €3.60/seat and 0.5kg. Comfort tuned via carrier flex zones. Benchmarked to Li Auto L8 integrated cushion carrier.

title: Direct-print decor on IP replacing applied trim appliqué
description: Apply the IP decor pattern by digital direct-print plus UV clearcoat onto the moulded substrate, removing the separately moulded-and-clipped appliqué panel and its 6 clips. Eliminates a part and its fit-gap, saving €3.90/vehicle, and enables low-cost decor variants without new tools. Benchmarked to Xpeng G9 direct-print IP decor.

title: Common cup-holder cartridge across console and door
description: Standardise a single cup-holder cartridge with spring-loaded retainer used in both the centre console and rear-door bin, replacing two unique designs. Doubles tool volume and removes a part number, saving €1.70/vehicle. Common retainer kinematics validated once. Benchmarked to AVATR 11 modular cup-holder cartridge.

title: Thermoformed mono-PET full IP topper for recyclability
description: Build the full upper-IP module as a thermoformed mono-PET skin over a PET-foam layer on a PET-GF substrate, fully recyclable as one stream and free of mixed adhesives. Removes the slush skin and PUR backfoam line, saving €6.80/vehicle and enabling 40% recyclate. Soft haptics via foam gauge. Benchmarked to Volvo mono-material IP strategy.

title: Single-shot IMG vent register bezel removing paint
description: Produce the vent-register bezel via in-mould-grain single-shot moulding, delivering the grained finish directly and removing the spray-paint and grain-etch steps. Eliminates two finishing operations per bezel, saving €2.10/vehicle across four registers and cutting VOC load. Benchmarked to Audi IMG interior trim adoption.

title: Integrated armrest-and-switchpack door module
description: Consolidate the door armrest, switch-pack carrier, and pull-handle bezel into a single co-moulded sub-module pre-assembled off-line, reducing main-line door-build from 9 parts to 4. Removes 5 clips and shortens line time 22 seconds, saving €3.50/door. Single connector to the door harness. Benchmarked to Luxeed S7 integrated door module.

title: Lightweight EPP dash insulator replacing EVA heavy-layer
description: Replace the EVA heavy-layer mass-spring dash insulator with a moulded EPP plus micro-perforated absorber tuned for the 800–4000Hz powertrain band, exploiting the lower noise floor of EV cabins. Cuts dash-insulator mass 45% (1.8kg) and material cost €5.20/vehicle while meeting articulation-index targets. Benchmarked to BYD Seal lightweight dash insulation.

title: Sprayable LASD floor damping replacing bitumen pads
description: Replace 14 die-cut bitumen damping pads with robot-applied liquid-applied sound deadener (LASD) sprayed to optimised thickness maps on the floor pan. Removes manual pad placement and heat-melt fixing, saving €6.10/vehicle and 1.6kg while improving coverage at structural anti-nodes. Benchmarked to Mercedes LASD floor-damping line.

title: Recycled-cotton-fibre body-cavity filler replacing PU foam
description: Replace expanding PU acoustic foam pillar fillers with pre-formed recycled-cotton-fibre baffles clipped into the cavity, removing the foam-expansion oven dwell and overspray cleanup. Saves €2.60/vehicle and uses recycled-content fibre for ESG scoring. Cavity sealing for wind-noise validated. Benchmarked to Volvo recycled-fibre cavity baffles.

title: Two-component moulded wheel-arch liner with integrated absorber
description: Combine the structural PP wheel-arch liner and the separately fixed felt absorber into one 2K-moulded liner with a needled-PET absorber layer fused in the tool. Removes the felt cut-and-clip operation and 6 fasteners per arch, saving €4.30/vehicle and improving tyre-cavity noise absorption. Benchmarked to Nio ET7 integrated arch-liner absorber.

title: Micro-perforated panel absorber replacing fibrous wadding
description: Replace bulky fibrous-wadding absorbers behind trim with thin micro-perforated panel (MPP) resonant absorbers tuned to dominant EV tonal frequencies. The 2mm MPP saves 30mm packaging and €3.10/vehicle in fibre while targeting absorption precisely. Recyclable single-material PP. Benchmarked to Zeekr MPP cabin absorbers.

title: Common floor-damping pad set across wheelbase variants
description: Standardise the floor-pan damping pad kit to one common set across short- and long-wheelbase variants by tuning a shared anti-node map, replacing two unique pad layouts. Consolidates die-cut tooling and removes part numbers, saving €2.40/vehicle. Damping loss-factor validated common. Benchmarked to Li Auto floor-pad commonisation.

title: Full floor acoustic package consolidation to single moulding
description: Consolidate the carpet, decoupler foam, and heavy-layer of the full floor acoustic package into a single moulded recycled-PET fibre stack with graded density, eliminating two separate layers and their assembly. Removes 1.3kg and €6.80/vehicle while meeting reverberation-time targets. Mono-material aids recycling. Benchmarked to BYD integrated floor acoustic module.

title: Clip-in tunable Helmholtz absorber for body cavities
description: Add lightweight clip-in Helmholtz resonator absorbers tuned to body-cavity boom modes, replacing mass-loaded foam dampers. The moulded PP resonator targets the 120–180Hz drumming band with minimal mass, saving €2.20/vehicle and 0.4kg versus broadband foam. Benchmarked to AVATR Helmholtz cavity tuning.

title: Robot-applied dash-insulator decoupler foam-in-place
description: Replace the moulded decoupler foam layer of the dash insulator with robot-dispensed foam-in-place applied directly to the firewall contour, eliminating the separate moulded part and its fit gaps. Improves sealing against pass-through leakage and saves €3.30/vehicle in scrap and inventory. Benchmarked to Maextro foam-in-place dash decoupler.

title: Mono-material PET dash-insulator absorber for recycling
description: Replace the mixed PU-foam-plus-PVC heavy-layer dash absorber with a mono-material recycled-PET fibre absorber, recyclable as one stream and free of PVC. Eliminates material separation and the plasticiser-fogging risk, saving €2.80/vehicle while meeting transmission-loss targets via graded fibre density. Benchmarked to Hongqi mono-material NVH absorbers.

title: Mould-in-colour PP-TD20 front bumper fascia delete paint shop
description: Replace painted ASA-capped fascia with mould-in-colour PP-TD20 (20% talc) in a self-coloured grain, deleting two-coat clear-coat priming. Eliminates ~€11/vehicle paint, masking and rework plus 0.9kg VOC abatement load, and removes one offline buffing station. UV-stabilised pigment masterbatch holds ΔE<1.2 over 3000h QUV. Cuts bumper cost ~9% across 220k units. Benchmarks BYD's mould-in-colour bumper strategy on Dolphin and Atto 3.

title: Thermoplastic SMC-free composite tailgate vs stamped steel
description: Convert two-piece stamped-steel liftgate to a moulded LFT-PP inner with PC/PBT outer, consolidating 6 stampings and 14 welds into 2 mouldings bonded by PUR adhesive. Saves 5.8kg per vehicle and removes spot-weld and e-coat steps, cutting ~€18/vehicle. Integrated spoiler and wiring bosses delete 4 brackets. Eliminates corrosion sealer. Benchmarks Volvo's composite tailgate on the EX30 platform.

title: PVD-coated bright trim replacing hexavalent chrome plating
description: Switch window-line and grille bright trim from electroplated chrome to PVD-sputtered tin/SST on PC/ABS, removing hexavalent-chrome line, effluent treatment and REACH authorisation cost. PVD cuts process cost ~€4.20/part and energy 35% while passing 480h CASS corrosion. Dark-satin and gun-metal finishes need no extra tooling. Benchmarks Audi's chrome-delete satin trim strategy on Q6 e-tron.

title: Single integrated front-end carrier module in-line assembly
description: Consolidate cooling pack, crash beam, headlamp brackets and AGS into one glass-reinforced PP-LGF40 front-end carrier delivered pre-assembled. Reduces 22 fasteners and 7 sub-brackets to a single 6-bolt fit, cutting line time 40s/vehicle and ~€14/vehicle. Hybrid metal inserts carry crash loads. Supplier sub-assembly shifts labour off main line. Benchmarks BMW's front-end module integration on Neue Klasse.

title: Active grille shutter electric actuator pin-count reduction
description: Replace 4-wire AGS actuator with a 2-wire LIN smart actuator carrying integrated position feedback, deleting two harness leads, one connector cavity and the discrete Hall sensor. Saves ~€1.90/vehicle in copper, terminals and assembly plus diagnostics over LIN. One PWM driver channel freed in the zonal controller. Benchmarks Porsche's LIN-actuated cooling-air-flap module on Macan EV.

title: Two-shot moulded badge replacing chromed emblem assembly
description: Replace adhesive-mounted chromed three-part emblem with a single 2K injection-moulded badge (clear PMMA over metallised PC) with integral snap clips, deleting plating, foam tape and a backing plate. Saves ~€2.10/badge and one assembly operation; snap-fit removes alignment fixture. Laser-clear illumination compatible. Benchmarks Mercedes' illuminated moulded star emblem approach.

title: Lower body cladding TPO downgauge with co-injection
description: Downgauge rocker and wheel-arch cladding from 2.8mm to 2.2mm using a higher-flow TPO (MFR 18) with recycled-core co-injection, cutting ~0.6kg and €2.40/vehicle resin while holding stone-impact and thermal-sag specs. Recycled core uses 30% PCR. Clip towers reinforced to retain pull-off force. Benchmarks Zeekr's lightweight cladding material strategy on 001.

title: Flush deployable door handle to fixed paddle simplification
description: Replace motorised flush-deploy handle with a fixed semi-flush mechanical paddle plus capacitive unlock, deleting the actuator motor, gear train, anti-pinch sensor and 5-wire harness per door. Saves ~€21/vehicle across four doors and removes a recurring warranty driver in cold climates. Frees four LIN nodes. Benchmarks Xpeng's simplified handle design on G6.

title: SMC-to-steel reversal on bumper beam using press-hardened boron
description: Replace aluminium-extrusion bumper beam with a press-hardened 22MnB5 roll-formed beam at 1.4mm, matching crash energy at lower material cost and deleting the MIG-welded crash-can interface in favour of bolted crush cans. Saves ~€7/vehicle versus aluminium and reuses existing PHS line. Benchmarks Hongqi's PHS crash-structure strategy on E-HS9.

title: Power-fold mirror delete to manual-fold with PVD glass
description: On base trim, replace power-fold mirror with manual-fold housing retaining heated PVD-coated glass, deleting fold motor, position sensor and 3-wire LIN feed per side. Saves ~€8/vehicle and two harness branches; heating stays on a single 2-wire feed. Reduces mirror mass 180g. Benchmarks Li Auto's trim-tiered mirror content strategy.

title: One-piece blow-moulded rear spoiler replacing bonded assembly
description: Convert two-shell ASA spoiler (upper/lower bonded with PUR) to a single gas-assist blow-moulded part with integral mounting studs, deleting the adhesive operation, fixture cure time and one shell tool. Saves ~€3.60/vehicle and 12% mass. Integral channel routes brake-lamp harness. Benchmarks Nio's integrated spoiler moulding on ET5.

title: Fender conversion from steel stamping to injection-moulded PA6
description: Replace stamped-steel front fenders with online-paintable PPE/PA blend mouldings, deleting two stamping dies, hemming and e-coat masking, and cutting 2.4kg/vehicle. Self-supporting geometry removes three reinforcement brackets and a corrosion concern. Saves ~€9/vehicle at 180k volume. Online-paint grade survives 200C e-coat bake. Benchmarks BMW's thermoplastic fender heritage carried to Neue Klasse.

title: Grille consolidation to single closed panel with radar window
description: Replace multi-slat open grille plus separate radar bezel with one closed body-colour panel incorporating a co-moulded radar-transparent window, deleting 9 chrome slats, a mesh insert and the bezel sub-assembly. Saves ~€6/vehicle and improves aero by 6 counts. EV closed face needs no airflow opening. Benchmarks Yangwang's sealed front-face design on U8.

title: Bumper energy absorber from EPP to ribbed PP structural foam
description: Replace moulded EPP pedestrian absorber with an injection-moulded ribbed PP structural-foam crash box tuned to the same lower-leg impact curve, deleting a separate bead-foam supplier and assembly clip-in step. Saves ~€2.80/vehicle and integrates into the fascia mould family. Benchmarks Denza's integrated absorber rib design on N7.

title: Full bumper system standardisation across two body styles
description: Carry one rear bumper system (fascia, beam, absorber, sensor brackets) across sedan and wagon by aligning lamp interfaces and tow-point, deleting one full tool set and halving sensor-bracket SKUs. Saves ~€13/vehicle blended plus €1.1M tooling avoidance. Common harness routing cuts two part numbers. Benchmarks Audi's modular bumper carryover across A6 variants.

title: Single-board rear lamp replacing PCB-plus-flex stack
description: Consolidate the rear-lamp PCB plus two flex tails into one rigid metal-core PCB hosting tail, stop, indicator and reverse LEDs, deleting two FFC connectors and a flex circuit per lamp. Saves ~€4.80/vehicle in flex, connectors and solder joints and improves thermal pull from LEDs. One LIN node per lamp retained. Benchmarks Mercedes' single-board rear-lamp architecture on EQE.

title: Shared LED driver across headlamp low/high/DRL channels
description: Replace three discrete constant-current drivers with one multi-channel buck driver IC time-sharing low-beam, high-beam and DRL strings, deleting two driver ICs, their inductors and EMC filters per headlamp. Saves ~€5.60/vehicle and 1.6W standby. Single SPI control reduces connector pins from 8 to 5. Benchmarks Audi's shared-driver Matrix LED module on Q6 e-tron.

title: Micro-LED matrix headlamp module replacing DMD projector
description: Replace 1.3MP DLP-DMD adaptive projector with a monolithic 25k-pixel micro-LED matrix module, deleting the DMD chip, relay optics and dedicated cooling fan while collapsing optics into one lens. Saves ~€34/vehicle at scale and 9W. Single high-density driver replaces three boards. Benchmarks Mercedes' Digital Light micro-LED roadmap.

title: DRL light-guide from machined PMMA to moulded PC light pipe
description: Switch signature DRL light guide from polished PMMA to optical-grade PC with moulded-in prism extraction features, deleting a secondary diamond-cutting step and improving thermal robustness near LEDs. Saves ~€2.30/vehicle and one fixture. Fewer LEDs needed (8 vs 11) for equal luminance via optimised coupling. Benchmarks Porsche's PC light-guide DRL on Taycan.

title: Rear lamp LED count reduction via secondary optics redesign
description: Redesign rear-lamp reflector cavities with a microlens collimating film to cut tail/stop LED count from 24 to 16 while meeting ECE R7 intensity, lowering LED BOM and driver current 30%. Saves ~€3.10/vehicle and 2.4W. Fewer solder joints raise first-pass yield. Benchmarks BYD's optimised rear-lamp optics on Seal.

title: Headlamp leveling stepper delete via static beam mapping
description: Remove the dynamic auto-leveling stepper motor and ride-height sensor on matrix variants by using pixel-level vertical cutoff control in software, deleting two motors, one sensor and a 4-wire feed per car. Saves ~€7/vehicle and frees ECU PWM channels. Software cutoff meets ECE R48 static aim. Benchmarks Audi's software-leveled Matrix headlamp.

title: Full lighting system controller centralisation to zonal node
description: Move headlamp and rear-lamp control logic out of four in-lamp ECUs into the front and rear zonal controllers, leaving only LED drivers in the lamps. Deletes 4 microcontrollers, their CAN transceivers and bootloaders, saving ~€11/vehicle and cutting lamp warranty returns. Lamps drop to LIN slaves. Benchmarks Xpeng's centralised lighting control on G9.

title: DRL and turn signal combined on shared multiplexed LED string
description: Combine white DRL and amber turn into RGBW LEDs on a single string driven by one channel with time-multiplexing, deleting a separate amber LED row and its driver lane. Saves ~€2.60/vehicle and reduces lamp depth 4mm. Meets ECE colorimetry on switchover. Benchmarks Hongqi's combined DRL/indicator signature on H9.

title: Headlamp housing from PC to filled PA6 for bracket integration
description: Switch headlamp housing from PC to 30% glass-filled PA6, raising stiffness so the three aiming brackets and a separate heat shield mould in directly, deleting four metal inserts and one assembly step. Saves ~€3.20/vehicle and 90g. Higher HDT tolerates LED hot-spots. Benchmarks Volvo's integrated headlamp housing on EX90.

title: Rear fog and reverse merged into single multifunction LED
description: Replace separate rear-fog and reverse modules with one dual-intensity white/red multifunction LED cluster in the bumper, deleting one reflector cavity, one driver lane and a connector. Saves ~€1.90/vehicle and one harness branch. Software arbitrates ECE-legal functions. Benchmarks AITO's consolidated rear signature on M7.

title: Matrix LED module count reduction via wider pixel pitch optics
description: Reduce matrix high-beam from 84 to 48 pixels using projection optics with finer angular resolution, holding glare-free performance while cutting LED and driver-channel count 43%. Saves ~€12/vehicle in emitters and silicon and 6W. One driver board removed. Benchmarks Audi's pixel-optimised Matrix on A6 e-tron.

title: Full headlamp system carryover with software-defined signatures
description: Standardise one hardware headlamp system across trims and differentiate DRL/welcome signatures purely in firmware, deleting three lamp variants and their tooling. Saves ~€10/vehicle blended plus €0.9M tool avoidance and simplifies aftersales to one part number. Addressable LED matrix enables per-trim animations. Benchmarks Zeekr's software-defined lighting strategy.

title: Aluminium body harness replacing copper on high-gauge runs
description: Convert main floor and roof harness branches above 2.5mm² from copper to aluminium with ultrasonic-welded Cu transition terminals, cutting ~2.1kg conductor mass and ~€14/vehicle at current Cu prices. Crimp creep mitigated by laser-welded ferrules. Targets 35% of harness copper. Benchmarks Mercedes' aluminium high-current harness on EQS.

title: Zonal E/E architecture replacing domain controllers
description: Migrate from seven domain ECUs to four zonal controllers plus a central compute, shortening harness by ~340m and ~9kg per vehicle while deleting ~20 ECUs through function consolidation. Cuts ~€48/vehicle in silicon, connectors and copper. Power and data ride short zonal stubs. Benchmarks Xpeng's zonal X-EEA 3.0 architecture on G6.

title: FFC flat-cable replacing round harness in door modules
description: Replace round multi-core door harness with laminated FFC flat cable terminating in ZIF connectors, deleting the rubber boot grommet, tape wrap and 60% of door-zone copper mass. Saves ~€7/vehicle and 280g per door, easing flush-handle and speaker routing. Benchmarks Nio's flat-cable door architecture on ET7.

title: Ring busbar power distribution replacing star fuse boxes
description: Replace two large star-topology fuse boxes and their thick feeders with a laser-welded aluminium ring busbar feeding zonal controllers, deleting ~40 blade fuses, two PDU housings and 4.5kg of feeder copper. Solid-state eFuses replace blades. Saves ~€26/vehicle. Benchmarks BMW Neue Klasse ring-busbar power topology.

title: Solid-state eFuse replacing blade fuse and relay banks
description: Replace 28 blade fuses and 9 electromechanical relays with high-side smart eFuse switches in the zonal PDU, deleting the relay carrier, fuse holders and manual fuse-pull service. Saves ~€9/vehicle, enables current diagnostics and removes a mechanical wear item. Programmable trip curves cut over-spec wiring. Benchmarks Zeekr's eFuse PDU on 001.

title: 48V zonal low-current distribution replacing 12V feeders
description: Move body and thermal loads to a 48V backbone, cutting conductor cross-section fourfold for equal power and saving ~5kg copper and ~€19/vehicle. Local 48-to-12V converters feed legacy loads. Lower I²R loss frees 220W. Thinner gauges ease routing. Benchmarks Tesla-style 48V topology mirrored by Zeekr's next-gen 48V architecture.

title: Central compute consolidating ADAS and infotainment SoCs
description: Fuse separate ADAS and IVI controllers into one central compute with partitioned SoCs sharing power, cooling and housing, deleting one ECU enclosure, its connectors and a 1Gb inter-ECU link. Saves ~€31/vehicle and 1.2kg, cutting two high-speed harnesses. Benchmarks Nio's Adam/central-compute consolidation on ET7.

title: CAN-FD backbone replacing dual-CAN plus gateway bridging
description: Collapse two classic-CAN buses and a bridging gateway into one CAN-FD backbone running 5Mbit, deleting the gateway ECU, its transceivers and a harness segment. Saves ~€8/vehicle and removes a latency hop. Fewer nodes lower termination-resistor count. Benchmarks AVATR's CAN-FD backbone on the 11.

title: Inline connector reduction via harness branch consolidation
description: Re-architect mid-floor harness to delete 14 inline splice connectors by routing through a single zonal junction, removing terminals, seals and a recurring connector-corrosion failure mode. Saves ~€5/vehicle in connector BOM and assembly clip time. Fewer mating cycles raise reliability. Benchmarks Li Auto's consolidated junction harness on L9.

title: Optical Ethernet backbone replacing shielded copper for cameras
description: Replace shielded copper coax camera links with a POF optical backbone for 8MP sensors, deleting heavy shielding, ferrites and EMC filtering while cutting 1.3kg and ~€11/vehicle. Immune to EMI, easing routing near inverters. Single fibre carries multiple streams. Benchmarks Mercedes' optical-backbone roadmap for high-bandwidth sensors.

title: Battery sense harness to integrated flex busbar CSC
description: Replace discrete cell-sense wires and rigid busbars with a laser-welded flex-PCB cell-supervision circuit integrating voltage taps, temperature pads and busbars, deleting ~90 sense leads and their connectors per pack. Saves ~€16/vehicle and 0.7kg. Benchmarks BYD's integrated flex CSC on Blade pack.

title: Roof zonal controller absorbing body-control functions
description: Add a compact roof zonal controller to absorb lighting, sunroof, mirror and antenna functions previously on a central BCM with long feeders, shortening interior harness ~70m and ~2.3kg. Local LIN stubs replace point-to-point runs. Saves ~€15/vehicle. Benchmarks Maextro's zonal interior architecture on the S800.

title: Full vehicle harness redesign to flat ribbon spine
description: Replace the conventional branched round harness with a flat laminated ribbon spine carrying power and LIN to zonal taps, deleting tape wrap, conduit and ~30% of connector cavities while enabling automated lay-flat assembly. Cuts ~€29/vehicle and 6kg. Benchmarks Luxeed's flat-harness spine on the R7.

title: Integrated coolant module octovalve replacing discrete valves
description: Consolidate eight three-way valves, two pumps and manifold hoses into one moulded octovalve coolant module with integrated pump and sensors, deleting ~14 hose joints, 9 clamps and a wiring branch. Saves ~€34/vehicle and 1.8kg, cutting leak paths and assembly time 90s. Benchmarks Tesla-style superbottle realised in Zeekr's integrated thermal module.

title: PTC cabin heater delete via heat-pump-only thermal loop
description: Remove the 7kW high-voltage PTC heater by extending heat-pump operation to -25C with vapour-injection and waste-heat recovery, deleting the PTC element, its HV connector and a coolant branch. Saves ~€26/vehicle and reclaims range. COP above 1.8 maintained. Benchmarks BYD's PTC-delete heat-pump strategy on Seal.

title: R290 propane refrigerant replacing R1234yf charge
description: Switch the heat-pump refrigerant from R1234yf to low-cost R290 propane, cutting per-charge refrigerant cost ~€7/vehicle and improving low-ambient COP. Hermetic secondary-loop architecture contains flammability per safety standard. Lower GWP eases compliance. Benchmarks Volvo's propane heat-pump development on SPA2.

title: Single-fluid loop unifying battery and cabin coolant circuits
description: Merge separate battery glycol and cabin chiller loops into one shared coolant circuit with a chiller plate interface, deleting one pump, one expansion tank and ~3m of hose. Saves ~€22/vehicle and 1.4kg, simplifying fill and bleed to a single service point. Benchmarks Tesla-style unified loop adopted in Li Auto's thermal design on L7.

title: Electric coolant pump downsizing via flow-optimised plumbing
description: Replace two 25W coolant pumps with one 30W pump after reducing hose bends and restrictions, cutting pump count, one LIN node and 20W parasitic draw. Saves ~€6/vehicle and a connector. Lower head loss meets battery flow target. Benchmarks Porsche's optimised single-pump battery loop on Taycan facelift.

title: Plastic-tank aluminium radiator to all-aluminium brazed core
description: Replace mechanically-crimped plastic-tank/aluminium radiator with a fully brazed all-aluminium core, deleting the nylon tank, gasket and crimp operation while cutting a long-term seep failure mode. Saves ~€3.40/vehicle and 240g and recovers material at end of life. Benchmarks Audi's brazed low-temp radiator on e-tron.

title: Chiller and EXV integration into coolant valve block
description: Integrate the battery chiller plate-heat-exchanger and its electronic expansion valve directly into the coolant valve block, deleting two refrigerant joints, a bracket and a mounting hose. Saves ~€13/vehicle and a refrigerant leak path, trimming assembly 40s. Benchmarks Denza's integrated chiller block on N7.

title: Three-way coolant valve actuator from BLDC to stepper LIN
description: Replace BLDC-driven coolant diverter valves with cost-down stepper-actuated LIN valves carrying integral position feedback, deleting the discrete driver IC and a sensor per valve. Saves ~€3.10/valve across four valves and standardises to one LIN node type. Benchmarks Hongqi's LIN coolant-valve standardisation on EHS7.

title: Full thermal loop integration into front cooling super-module
description: Consolidate the complete front thermal loop, radiators, condenser, compressor mounts, valve block and pumps, into one pre-charged cooling super-module fitted as a single unit, deleting ~24 hose connections and shifting plumbing labour off-line. Saves ~€38/vehicle and 130s line time plus leak-test simplification. Benchmarks AVATR's integrated front thermal module on 12.

title: Heat-pump compressor sharing across cabin and battery duty
description: Use one variable-speed scroll compressor time-shared between cabin AC and battery chilling via valve sequencing instead of dual compressors, deleting one compressor, its HV inverter and mount. Saves ~€42/vehicle and 4kg while cutting one HV connector. Software arbitrates priority. Benchmarks Maextro's single-compressor thermal architecture on S800.
